paper.title: P3D-Bench: Benchmarking MLLMs for Parametric 3D Generation and Structural Reasoning
paper.abstract: Multimodal large language models can write code to produce complex programs as well as use programs to do 3D modeling, which opens up a new avenue for 3D generation powered by their priors, world knowledge and reasoning. Yet existing benchmarks rarely evaluate 3D modeling through code. Such modeling demands more than runnable code: from a text or visual specification, a model must generate a parametric 3D program that is geometrically precise, semantically aligned and assembly-consistent. We introduce P3D-Bench, a benchmark for parametric 3D generation. Unlike a 3D mesh, a parametric 3D program exposes explicit dimensions, construction operations and part relations, revealing whether a model recovers a design's structure, not just its appearance. Under a unified protocol, P3D-Bench covers three task families (Text-to-3D, Image-to-3D and Assembly-3D) and scores each output for executability, geometric fidelity, topology, text-grounded constraints, multiview semantic alignment and part-level structure. We evaluate frontier MLLMs and text-only LLMs on 400 text cases, 400 image cases and 203 annotated assemblies, with domain-specific models as reference points. Our extensive evaluation yields three findings. First, assemblies are the hardest setting, where models still fail to compose multiple parts into a coherent structure. Second, models can often recover the global shape and semantic identity of the target object, yet fail to reproduce the precise parametric geometry specified by the input. Third, part-level modeling remains weak on assemblies, where models recover neither the geometry of each part nor the right number of parts. These results position P3D-Bench as a benchmark for evaluating precise parametric geometry and part-level structure in parametric 3D generation. Project page: https://lucasqaq.github.io/p3d/.

runs.condition: Use a main base declared size of length 0.75, width 0.21875, and height 0.28125, with main extrusion depth 0.2812. Form the base as one solid upper-side extrusion from the base datum, filling the footprint to the left, right, front, and back edges with offsets of 0, and keep the side outline arched rather than reducing it to a rectangular notch. Add one remove-material feature made only from 2 separate circular profiles, not a connected slot or rectangular pocket. Place one circular axis 0.0717 from the left edge and 0.0625 from the front edge, and place the other circular axis 0.6692 from the left edge and 0.0625 from the front edge; each circular profile has radius 0.0312. Cut from the upper side with the removed band running from 0.1081 to 0.1705 above the base underside, using cut depth 0.0625. Treat the 0.6599 by 0.0625 span only as the envelope of the circular pattern, leaving the surrounding and intervening material solid.

The main piece is a single solid extrusion with a long flat top and a rounded arched lower outline, not a stack of separate blocks. The visible underside void should remain curved, with straight vertical solid portions at the ends and a continuous slab across the top. In addition to the arched body outline, there is a paired circular material-removal pattern placed on the upper side; those circular features are cuts or recesses, not raised bosses. The circular removals use a shallower depth than the main body, so the model has a stepped-depth relationship while the surrounding material stays solid.

Use a main base reference span of length 0.75, width 0.421875, and height 0.191921, with the lower base extrusion extending 0.1919 upward from the base datum. Form this lower base from separate rounded profiles within the full 0.75 by 0.4218 span rather than as a filled rectangular plate: place one circular profile with its axis 0.2109 from the left edge and 0.2109 from the front edge, radius 0.2109, and place a second concentric circular group with its axis 0.6094 from the left edge and 0.2109 from the front edge, using radius 0.1406 with an inner radius of 0.1055 for the opening. Preserve the additional curved and slot-like profile portions in the lower sketch as rounded cut or boundary shapes, so the openings remain true voids rather than surface markings. Add the taller circular solid section over the left-side circular axis within a 0.4218 by 0.4218 footprint, offset 0 from the left edge, 0.3282 from the right edge, and 0 from both front and back edges; this upper-side material reaches 0.4424 from the same base datum, making it a taller continuation above the shallow base rather than an added segment stacked on top of the base height. Keep the upper circular section hollow/annular where the inner loop is present, including the rounded slot-like interruptions in the opening. Add a separate solid rectangular upright tab with length 0.041188, width 0.070313, and height 0.441346; position it with left offset 0.0703, right offset 0.6385, front offset 0.1758, and back offset 0.1757, and extrude it upward 0.4413 from the base datum.

The model is built from rounded circular sections with real central openings, so the visible collars should read as hollow/annular rather than solid disks. A lower rounded base links the larger and smaller circular areas, with curved outlines rather than a simple rectangular plate between them. The taller round section rises from one side of the lower form and has an inner opening with slot-like rounded interruptions at the top. A narrow solid rectangular upright tab is added beside the tall round body and reaches nearly the same height, while the lower base remains much shallower.

Add two upper-side solid narrow rectangular edge strips over this rear region, each with footprint 0.0083 by 0.5 and main extrusion depth 0.0083; one strip is flush with the left edge and offset 0.4917 from the right edge, and the other is flush with the right edge and offset 0.4917 from the left edge, with both offset 0.25 from the front and flush with the back. Add the thin upper-side transverse solid feature with footprint 0.5 by 0.250819, flush to the left and right edges, offset 0.24922 from the front edge and 0.249961 from the back edge; its own thickness is 0.0021 and its total reach from the base datum is 0.335265. Preserve the closed internal profile in the added material as a real open void through that local solid. For the aligned stepped portion, treat the upper pad depth as 0.1 and the deeper continuation below it as a separate solid continuation with own depth 0.2167; the deeper footprint is contained within the shallow footprint, shares the left, front, and back edges, and its reach is measured from the same shoulder rather than added end-to-end.

The model is built from a thin rectangular base slab with all other visible features added as solid material rather than cut away from it. A larger raised rectangular portion occupies the rear area of the base, leaving a front strip of the base exposed. Narrow raised strips run along the left and right sides of that raised area, and a very thin transverse added feature spans across the width with a real internal opening rather than a surface mark. The vertical extents are stepped: some added material is shallow while one aligned continuation reaches deeper from the same shoulder, so the underside is not a single flat level.

Start with the main reference body as a straight rectangular rail 0.748506 long, 0.024642 wide, and 0.018482 high, made as a solid extrusion of depth 0.0185 from the base datum. Add a second solid layer over the full same footprint of the rail, 0.748506 by 0.024642, with thickness 0.00462 and extrusion depth 0.0046, raising the upper surface to 0.0231 from the base datum. In the rail, cut a row of two separate circular recesses of radius 0.0043; use a common center offset of 0.0108 from the front edge, with the centers 0.0138 and 0.7346 from the left end. These circular cuts are removed material only, with their vertical band from 0.0062 to 0.0185 above the base underside and an own cut depth of 0.0123, leaving lower material rather than passing through the full rail height.

At the right end, add a solid equal-sided end block 0.030803 by 0.030803 with the same lower-body height 0.018482 and extrusion depth 0.0185. Position this block with its left edge 0.7192 from the rail left end, its right edge 0.0015 beyond the rail right end, its front edge 0.0361 in front of the rail front edge, and its back edge 0.0299 beyond the rail back edge. Add a matching shallow solid top layer over this end block, also 0.030803 by 0.030803, with thickness 0.00462 and extrusion depth 0.0046. In the end block, cut a circular recess of radius 0.0043 with its center 0.7361 from the rail left end and 0.0192 in front of the rail front edge; this removed circular band runs from 0.0062 to 0.0185 above the base underside with depth 0.0123.

Also remove an equal-sided rectangular cutout 0.027722 by 0.027722 in the end block, sharing the end block left edge and front edge. This cut is a deeper removed section, not an added pad, with a total reach of 0.077 from the upper lower-body shoulder: its vertical band runs from 0.0185 above the base underside down to 0.0585 below the underside. Preserve the stepped relationship between the shallow 0.0046 top layers, the 0.0123 circular recess bands, the 0.0185 lower solid body, and the deeper 0.077 end cutout.

The part is dominated by a very long, slim rectangular rail with a shallow full-length top layer, so it is not just a single-height strip. Small circular removed features sit near the rail ends along the same narrow band; these are recesses or cuts, not raised posts. One end has a wider block-like extension with its own matching shallow top layer and additional removed features. The end detailing includes both shallow circular recessing and a much deeper cutout, creating a stepped depth relationship through and below the lower body.

The part is built from a rectangular base slab with added solid material forming a stepped back strip and an upper raised area. The vertical and overhanging-looking sections are attached solids, not cutouts, and one deeper strip continues farther from the base than the shallower raised circular feature. The top circular feature should be treated as an annular collar around a smaller void, not as a solid disk.

Start with the main rectangular base as the reference: length 0.703125, width 0.75, height 0.013125, with the base extrusion depth 0.0131. In this base plate, make one circular through-opening with its axis 0.075 from the left edge and 0.6562 from the front edge, radius 0.0328. Add the main rectangular block on the upper side of the base with length 0.646875, width 0.50625, and height 0.645; place its footprint with left offset 0.0563, right offset -0.0001, front offset 0.0844, and back offset 0.1594. On the block top, add two separate annular collars, not a connecting pad: each collar has outer radius 0.0656, inner radius 0.0328, and height 0.0375, with axes at left offset 0.2062 and front offsets 0.1968 and 0.4781. Cut two shallow rectangular recesses into the top face of the block; their combined bounding span is length 0.473437 by width 0.39375, positioned with left offset 0.1734, right offset 0.0563, front offset 0.1406, and back offset 0.2157. These rectangular recesses remove only the top band from 0.6581 down to 0.6544 relative to the base underside, giving a cut depth of 0.0037, so they are blind recesses rather than through-cuts. Also keep the collar centers open with circular cuts of radius 0.0328 at left offset 0.2062 and front offsets 0.1969 and 0.4781; these openings pass through the 0.0375 collar height and continue only through the same shallow top band, not through the full 0.645 block. The 0.0375 collar tier sits above the main block top, while the 0.645 rectangular block is the deeper continuation down to the top of the 0.0131 base plate.

A thin rectangular base plate supports a taller rectangular solid that is set in from some edges and nearly flush to one side. The top face has shallow rectangular cuts, and the pair of round features are added annular collars with open centers rather than solid disks. The collar openings continue only into the shallow top band and should not be modeled as holes through the full height of the block. The base remains a thin slab with a separate round through-opening near the projecting end, while the taller block and collars create the stepped vertical form.

Cut the coaxial stepped bore/recess below the collar as circular removed material only, not as a rectangular pocket. Use the same axis at left offset 0.1583 and front offset 0.3113, with circular removed profiles of radius 0.0748 and radius 0.053; the main recessed band runs from 0.0218 to 0.0944 above the base underside with cut depth 0.0726 and a circular face envelope of 0.1496 by 0.1496. Viewed in the render, the assembly presents a wedge-shaped silhouette: a bulbous, multi-faceted cap on the left transitions into a thin, flat fin plate that tapers to a sharp point on the right. Five circular openings are arranged in a line along the top ridge of the fin, a concave arc cutout sweeps along one side to clear the hub cap, and two small wire rings protrude from the lower face and the pointed tip. The single-instance fin plate (part 98bbd162, role 'fin_plate') is the largest component, with approximate extents of 21.85 × 11.28 × 1.5 mm, an aspect ratio of 14.57, and a surface inventory of 34 conical faces (45° chamfer strips and conical bore profiles), 20 planar faces, 17 cylindrical faces, and 2 B-spline surfaces forming the freeform outer contour. Its holes_on_part list is empty because the openings carry conical chamfer transitions rather than pure cylindrical bores. Two further part classes each appear in 2 instances and form the 2 detected mirror pairs: one pair likely constitutes the two half-shells of the rounded hub cap visible at the blunt end, and the other pair corresponds to the two small toroidal rings. A fourth unique part (1 instance) may be the inner cylindrical boss or bearing seat visible at the hub centre. All 6 instances are grounded. Contact evidence totals 10 face-to-face contacts — 4 mating-plane interfaces (flat-face abutments, principally the fin plate to hub shells), 3 coaxial cylindrical fits (bore-to-bore or bore-to-shaft alignments at the hub and openings), and 3 freeform contacts (chamfered cone surfaces meeting B-spline shells). The surface-type histogram across the full assembly — Cylinder 44, Plane 41, Cone 36, BSplineSurface 34, Torus 12 — reflects the extensive chamfering, the freeform shell curvatures, and the toroidal blends or ring features. No fastener-through-hole stacks are inferred; the five openings in the fin remain unoccupied.

Parts:
- fin_plate: A thin, wing-shaped plate approximately 21.85 × 11.28 × 1.5 mm (aspect ratio ~14.6) featuring a pointed tapered tip, a concave arc cutout on one side, and a row of five circular openings along the upper edge plus one opening near the lower body. Its 34 conical faces indicate extensive 45° chamfering along perimeter edges and conical bore profiles in the openings. The part interfaces with neighbours through 2 plane-on-plane contacts at its flat faces, 3 cylinder-on-cylinder coaxial fits through bore features, and 3 cone-on-NURBS contacts. Being grounded with a single instance, it likely serves as a structural fin or mounting bracket, where the openings could receive fasteners and the concave cutout appears to clear a cylindrical element.

Annotation Caveats:
Three of the four unique part classes (98c23a00, 98c2af48, 98bce328) returned null extraction status and empty descriptions/holes, so their geometry is inferred solely from the rendered image, contact evidence, and surface-type histograms at the assembly level. The fin plate's openings are not registered as holes_on_part because their bore profiles include conical chamfers rather than pure cylinders, and no fastener bodies pass through them (hole_edges_summary is empty). The identity of the two mirror-paired part classes — as hub-cap half-shells and small wire rings — is a visual inference, not confirmed by part dossiers.

The single-instance fin plate (part 98bbd162, role 'fin_plate') is the largest component, with approximate extents of 21.85 × 11.28 × 1.5 mm, an aspect ratio of 14.57, and a surface inventory of 34 conical faces (45° chamfer strips and conical bore profiles), 20 planar faces, 17 cylindrical faces, and 2 B-spline surfaces forming the freeform outer contour. Its holes_on_part list is empty because the openings carry conical chamfer transitions rather than pure cylindrical bores. Two further part classes each appear in 2 instances and form the 2 detected mirror pairs: one pair likely constitutes the two half-shells of the rounded hub cap visible at the blunt end, and the other pair corresponds to the two small toroidal rings. A fourth unique part (1 instance) may be the inner cylindrical boss or bearing seat visible at the hub centre. All 6 instances are grounded. Contact evidence totals 10 face-to-face contacts — 4 mating-plane interfaces (flat-face abutments, principally the fin plate to hub shells), 3 coaxial cylindrical fits (bore-to-bore or bore-to-shaft alignments at the hub and openings), and 3 freeform contacts (chamfered cone surfaces meeting B-spline shells). The surface-type histogram across the full assembly — Cylinder 44, Plane 41, Cone 36, BSplineSurface 34, Torus 12 — reflects the extensive chamfering, the freeform shell curvatures, and the toroidal blends or ring features. No fastener-through-hole stacks are inferred; the five openings in the fin remain unoccupied.

Viewed in the render, the assembly reads as a stylised chair: a tall pointed backrest panel rises roughly 215 mm above a broader seat platform, while four waisted legs descend about 100 mm below. The overall bounding box measures 110 × 300 × 100 mm with an aspect ratio of 3.0 along the tallest axis, and the total volume is approximately 440 384 mm³ distributed across 159 faces (108 planar, 49 cylindrical, 2 B-spline), 381 edges, and 232 vertices. The tree depth is 1 (flat hierarchy, no sub-assemblies), and none of the 5 instances is grounded.

The single decorative bracket (part 3b67f32e, 1 instance) is the largest body, measuring roughly 100 × 110 × 215 mm. Its vertical plate carries a pointed-leaf contour defined by the two B-spline surfaces, with several diamond-shaped through-cutouts whose wall normals are tilted approximately 30° from the primary axes. The horizontal base includes semicircular arc features (R ≈ 50 mm) and additional curved contours (R ≈ 20 mm, R ≈ 10 mm). Four leg parts — two mirrored column_spacers (part 3b66e190, 2 instances), one column (part 3b677db8, 1 instance), and one column_spacer (part 3b666c40, 1 instance) — each occupy a 25 × 25 × 100 mm envelope with an aspect ratio of 4.0. Their side faces are large-radius concave cylinders (R ≈ 130–145 mm) producing the distinctive waisted silhouette, with R = 2 mm fillet rounds at vertical edges. All 16 contact edges in the assembly are mating-plane pairs; no hole-based fastener connections exist. The layout_tags report one mirror_pair, corresponding to the two identical column_spacer instances symmetrically placed beneath the seat.

- column: A slender solid column measuring approximately 25 × 25 × 100 mm (aspect ratio 4.0) with a square cross-section at top and bottom and concave cylindrical side faces (radii ~130 and ~145 mm) that give it a waisted profile mid-height. Small 2.0 mm-radius fillets round the vertical corners, and angled planar chamfers flare the cross-section at each end. The part has no holes and participates in 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts through its flat end and chamfer faces. It likely serves as a structural column or post element that mates between flat surfaces.

The top and bottom faces are flat planes, and the part participates in 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts, indicating it likely functions as a spacer or standoff post that mates with flat surfaces at each end.
- column_spacer (×2): A 25 × 25 × 100 mm solid column (aspect ratio 4.0) with a square footprint and four gently concave longitudinal sides formed by large-radius cylindrical surfaces (R ≈ 130–145 mm), giving it a waisted profile that narrows toward the mid-height. - decorative_bracket: An L-shaped bracket-like solid approximately 100 × 110 × 215 mm (aspect ratio 2.15), consisting of a thin vertical plate rising from a horizontal base, with a fill ratio of only about 10 % of its bounding box. The vertical plate carries a pointed-leaf B-spline contour and several diamond-shaped through-cutouts, while the base features semicircular arcs (R ≈ 50 mm) on its sides and additional diamond cutouts on its top face. The part mates with neighbors through 16 plane-on-plane contacts across its multiple flat faces and has no cylindrical holes. It likely serves as a structural or decorative bracket, with the diamond cutouts possibly permitting tab engagement or serving as weight-reduction features.

In the rendered image the assembly stands vertically, roughly 800 mm tall and 277 mm across, with a flat disc plate at the top, a long thin rod descending from it, and a barrel-shaped cage of slats at the lower end. The grounded part is the disc plate (180 mm diameter × 10 mm thick, one central 10 mm through-hole), which serves as the topmost anchor. The rod is a featureless solid cylinder of 10 mm diameter and 500 mm length (aspect ratio 50) that passes through the disc plate's central bore; hole inference confirms this link with zero perpendicular misalignment and 0.3 mm axial overlap. At the lower end of the rod, a flanged bushing (56.5 mm outer flange diameter, 40 mm through-bore, 11.25 mm tall) and a bushing/locating plug (20 mm main-body radius, 23 mm rim radius, 77 mm tall with a conical lower taper and a blind 24 mm bore at the top) are linked coaxially through a second hole inference edge (diameter 40 mm, zero perpendicular misalignment, 11.85 mm axial overlap matching the bushing's full height). These hub components sit at the top of the cage. A splined ring (200 mm diameter, 3.9 mm thick, 40 external teeth, inner bore ~160 mm) encircles the hub zone; its 40 teeth match the 40 vane slats in count. Each vane slat is an extremely thin panel (~3.9 × 50 × 300 mm, aspect ratio 76.9) with a gentle outward curvature (cylinder radius 572.5 mm on one face) and two edge notches that likely engage with the splined ring's teeth for location and retention. A layout tag confirms a 40-fold rotational arrangement of the slats about the Y-axis, centered at approximately y = −80.4 mm. Completing the assembly is a knob (60 mm wide, 81 mm tall) with a spherical upper dome and conical stem that appears to cap the hub. One part (3f1c184a, single instance) lacks geometric data and may serve as an additional connector or spacer in the hub region.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents an elongated warship figurine whose hull tapers symmetrically fore and aft, with the longest dimension running approximately 100 mm. Three squat cylindrical bosses (radius 5.0 mm each) rise from the deck as turret bases, two flanking the central superstructure and one farther forward or aft; a smaller cylinder (radius 2.5 mm) projects upward from the superstructure as a chimney-like element. All 11 instances are grounded, and the assembly tree is flat (depth 1) with no nested sub-assemblies.

The hull body (part c502ddfe, 1 instance) accounts for the vast majority of the assembly's 75 faces, carrying 39 planar faces (deck, base, superstructure walls, and angled chamfer transitions at ±45°), 4 cylindrical faces, and 1 BSplineSurface that wraps the hull's lower contour, producing 3 elliptical transition edges. Nine pin parts (c5037a46) are solid cylinders of diameter 1.0 and length 10.0, each with just 3 faces; these correspond to the triple gun-barrel clusters visible at each turret. A single spacer part (c5054f40) is a partial-cylindrical segment (bbox ~2.1 × 6.2 × 9.7, 4 faces including 1 BSpline surface and 2 oblique planar end caps with elliptical edges) that mates to the hull via 2 of the assembly's 2 total mating-plane contacts. The layout is tagged as a linear chain along the Z-axis (the ship's length), consistent with the fore-to-aft arrangement of turrets and superstructure. No hole edges or fastener-through-hole stacks were detected; the pins appear to be placed into the turret bosses without recorded cylindrical contact pairs.

Viewed from above and slightly to one side, the assembly presents as a low-profile, organic, pebble-shaped enclosure with a visible circumferential parting line dividing an upper and a lower dome, and a thin disc-like element visible at the seam. The layout is tagged as a linear chain along the Y-axis, reflecting the vertical stacking of the three unique, single-instance parts. The base cover (part 4607dfae) is the largest body at roughly 303.9 × 159.5 × 72.0 mm with an aspect ratio of 4.22; its geometry comprises 9 B-spline surfaces forming the curved walls and 31 planar faces for the flat base and small embossed top-deck details, plus 4 cylindrical faces at a radius of 6.0 mm with an 8.0 mm axial extent that correspond to a small circular boss or recess on the top deck. The housing cover (part 461c9fae) is somewhat smaller at 227.4 × 119.7 × 50.0 mm (aspect ratio 4.55) and is overwhelmingly freeform, with 292 B-spline faces and 19 planar faces; its upper surface features an oval recessed panel containing five small circular-edge dot features and several embossed relief symbols arranged in a cross pattern, clearly visible in the render. The cover plate (part 468e7438) is an extremely thin oval disc at 241.82 × 126.86 × 6.0 mm (aspect ratio ~40:1) with only 3 faces — two opposing planar faces and one B-spline rim — and no holes, bosses, or other features. No contact edges of any surface-pair type were recorded in the assembly graph, and no hole-link inferences exist because none of the three parts carry any through-holes or blind holes. All three parts are individually grounded. The absence of any detected mating interfaces suggests the parts nest concentrically by geometry alone, possibly retained by snap-fit detents or adhesive bonds that are not modeled as contact surfaces in the CAD data.

Parts:
- base_cover: A smooth, elongated, pebble-shaped dome with an oval footprint roughly 304 mm × 159 mm × 72 mm (aspect ratio ~4.2). - housing_cover: A smooth, elongated dome-shaped solid approximately 227 mm × 120 mm × 50 mm (aspect ratio 4.55), composed almost entirely of freeform BSpline surfaces (292 of 311 faces) with 19 planar faces oriented strictly up or down. - cover_plate: A very thin, flat, oval-shaped solid measuring roughly 242 × 127 × 6 mm with an aspect ratio of about 40:1. The two large flat faces with opposing normals represent the primary potential mating surfaces. Given its featureless, thin disc form, this part likely serves as a cover plate or closure element that could seat flush against or into a corresponding oval recess.

The part has no holes, no cylindrical faces, and no recorded assembly contacts, suggesting it likely serves as an outer housing cover or enclosure shell that could mate to a lower body through snap-fit or similar means.
- cover_plate: A very thin, flat, oval-shaped solid measuring roughly 242 × 127 × 6 mm with an aspect ratio of about 40:1. It consists of just two parallel planar faces (top and bottom) joined by a smooth B-spline peripheral rim — only 3 faces total — with no holes, bosses, or cylindrical features whatsoever. The two large flat faces with opposing normals represent the primary potential mating surfaces. Given its featureless, thin disc form, this part likely serves as a cover plate or closure element that could seat flush against or into a corresponding oval recess.

Annotation Caveats:
No contact edges of any type were detected between any pair of parts, despite the three shells clearly being designed to nest together. All three parts are independently grounded, so there is no parent-child kinematic chain. No holes or fastener features exist on any part, meaning retention mechanisms (snap-fits, adhesive, friction) are not captured in the geometry. The 4 cylindrical faces on the base cover (r = 6 mm) may represent a locating boss but no corresponding mating feature was found on the housing cover. The embossed text and icon reliefs are modeled as surface features only, without volumetric detail.

The rendered image shows an articulated arm in a raised-elbow pose, extending from a wide circular base at the lower left up and to the right where a two-jaw gripper is visible in an open configuration. The overall bounding box spans 830 × 732 × 403 mm with an aspect ratio of 2.06. The single grounded part is the housing cap (56d3daba), a disc-like body approximately 390.5 × 390.5 × 104 mm with a stepped cylindrical profile (outer radius 150 mm, inner ledge at 130 mm radius), a central locating boss at 15 mm radius, and an array of peripheral mounting lugs whose radially-oriented flat faces produce numerous plane normals at 30° intervals. Attached above this base is the lever arm with bevel gear (56d86e68, 180 × 228 × 323 mm), featuring concentric cylindrical steps at 125, 90, and 68.5 mm radius forming the turntable interface, and 24 radially arranged tooth features at its upper hub suggesting a bevel gear mesh. The connecting arm (56b8152e, 442.5 × 120 × 62 mm, aspect ratio 7.14) is the long curved link visible in the image, with hub bores at each end (radius 15 mm) and a bolted flange carrying 13 blind holes of diameter 6 mm. The joint housing (56e65190, 93.9 × 132.5 × 296 mm) provides the elbow joint with its L-shaped geometry, two perpendicular blind bores of 30 mm diameter, a 44 mm through-bore, and a 12-hole bolt-circle flange. The multi-bore housing (56e42e6e, 76.2 × 80 × 173.1 mm) carries the characteristic multi-lobed profile visible in the forearm, with a large cross-bore at 85 mm radius and longitudinal bores at 35.4 mm radius. The scroll housing (56e3b934, 87.6 × 70 × 55.4 mm) sits at the wrist with a circular rim of 35 mm radius and four through-holes for mounting. Two gripper jaws (56d365ba, 19.5 × 70.9 × 126.9 mm each) form a mirror pair with serrated gripping faces, a pivot boss at radius 15 mm, and elongated adjustment slots. Twelve spacer rings (56de3b2e, 6 × 6 × 3 mm each, bore diameter 3.2 mm) are arranged in a 12-fold rotational pattern whose axis aligns approximately with the base's vertical, likely functioning as bearing spacers or roller-simulating elements in the turntable joint. Contact-edge counts total 20 mating-plane and 6 coaxial-fit interfaces, with 4 ambiguous contacts, and no fastener-through-hole stacks were inferred (consistent with all bolt holes being blind). Layout tags confirm a roughly planar part arrangement, the 12-fold rotational cluster of spacers, and a single mirror pair corresponding to the two gripper jaws.

Parts:
- housing_cap: A squat circular body approximately 390 mm in diameter and 104 mm tall (aspect ratio ~3.76), featuring a stepped top deck with a small central cylindrical boss (radius 15 mm, height ~20 mm) and multiple radial lugs distributed symmetrically around the outer cylindrical wall (radius 150 mm). An inner concentric cylindrical step at 130 mm radius creates a recessed ledge. The part participates in 2 plane-on-plane and 1 coaxial cylinder-on-cylinder contact with neighboring components. Being a single grounded instance with no through-holes, it likely serves as a housing cap or cover that locates concentrically via its cylindrical fits and could be retained by the engagement of its peripheral lugs.
- lever_arm_with_bevel_gear: An elongated lever arm (aspect ratio ~1.80, bbox ~180 × 228 × 323 mm) with a stepped cylindrical disc base, a web-like connecting arm, and an upper hub featuring 24 radially arrayed tooth-like faces at radius 50 mm consistent with a bevel or crown gear profile. Two perpendicular blind holes of 30 mm diameter and 20 mm depth appear to serve as shaft or pin seats. The part engages its neighbors through 4 plane-on-plane and 2 cylinder-on-cylinder contact pairs, and likely functions as a pivoting lever or actuator arm that transmits rotary motion through the toothed hub coupling.
- spacer (×12): A thick-walled cylindrical ring with an outer diameter of 6.0 mm, an inner through-bore of approximately 3.2 mm diameter, and an axial length of 3.0 mm (aspect ratio 2.0). The geometry comprises only an outer cylinder, an inner bore, and two flat annular end faces. With 12 instances in the assembly and a through-hole that could accommodate a shaft or fastener, the part likely serves as a spacer or bushing providing axial separation between adjacent components.
- connecting_arm: An elongated curved arm (aspect ratio ~7.1, approximately 442 mm long) with a cylindrical hub at each end; the larger hub carries a flanged bolt circle of thirteen 6 mm-diameter blind holes while both hubs feature central bores of roughly 30 mm diameter. The part mates in its assembly through 4 plane-on-plane and 2 cylinder-on-cylinder contacts, indicating both bores likely receive coaxial cylindrical partners. Two or three small rectangular lugs protrude from the top of the curved shank. The geometry suggests this part likely functions as a connecting arm or lever linking two parallel rotational axes.
- joint_housing: An L-shaped solid housing approximately 296 mm long (aspect ratio ~3.15), comprising a chamfered blocky head and a cylindrical arm (outer diameter ~62 mm) with a long internal bore of diameter 44 mm running 200 mm through the arm. Two perpendicular blind bores of 30 mm diameter and 20 mm depth appear on the housing faces, and twelve shallow 3 mm-diameter holes are arranged in a bolt-circle on the flange joining the arm to the housing. The part participates in 3 plane-on-plane and 2 cylinder-on-cylinder coaxial contacts in the assembly. It appears to serve as a right-angle joint housing that could permit rotary motion about two perpendicular axes.
- multi_bore_housing: An elongated housing body approximately 173 × 80 × 76 mm (aspect ratio ≈ 2.27) with a distinctive wavy, multi-lobed profile created by 76 B-spline curve edges. It features a large Ø 170 mm through-bore in the cross-direction, several smaller Ø ≈ 71 mm bores running along its length, and a Ø 30 mm protruding cylindrical boss on one end. Around the perimeter, approximately 22 counterbored bolt positions (Ø 8 / 4.5 / 2.5 mm stepped features) secure the housing, and two Ø 20 mm blind holes on opposite faces likely serve as locating-pin seats. Assembly contacts include 3 plane-on-plane and 2 cylinder-on-cylinder pairs, suggesting this part likely functions as a multi-bore housing that mates via flat parting surfaces and coaxial fits with adjacent components.
- knob_plug: A stepped body approximately 85 × 85 × 104 mm (aspect ratio 1.222) featuring a large dome-shaped head formed by B-spline surfaces, a narrow peripheral groove at two closely spaced radii (~41.25 and ~39.5 mm), a cylindrical shank (R = 20 mm, 24 mm long) that participates in one cylinder-on-cylinder coaxial fit, and a squared rear section with four flat side faces that could serve as an anti-rotation key. Three plane-on-plane contacts indicate shoulder and/or square-end mating with neighboring parts. The part appears to function as a knob or stepped plug whose head provides a flange or grip surface while the square tail likely prevents rotation.

- scroll_housing: A compact housing body approximately 87.6 × 70.0 × 55.4 mm (aspect ratio ~1.58) featuring a large circular front rim at radius 35.0 mm enclosing a scroll-shaped internal cavity with an inner wall at radius 23.5 mm. The rear section is angular with stepped mounting flanges carrying four through-holes of diameter 3.0 mm and a blind bore of diameter 20.0 mm that could accept a bearing or locating pin. It participates in six plane-on-plane and one cylinder-on-cylinder assembly contacts, suggesting it mates via multiple flat faces and one coaxial cylindrical fit. This part likely serves as a scroll-type housing that encloses a rotating element and bolts to an adjacent structure.
- gripper_jaw (×2): An angular, V-shaped body (aspect ratio 6.505, extents ~19.5 × 70.9 × 126.9 mm) with two arm segments meeting at an obtuse angle, a serrated sawtooth edge at one tip, two elongated oblong slots (slot-end radii ~6–7.5 mm) along the connecting arm, a large cylindrical boss (R = 15 mm), and a Ø3 mm through-hole along the thin axis. The part participates in 4 plane-on-plane contacts and appears in two instances in the assembly, suggesting a symmetric pair. The combination of a pivot boss, adjustment slots, serrated gripping face, and paired instances indicates this part likely serves as a gripper jaw or clamping finger that could pivot and engage objects via the serrations.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented trophy form roughly 60 × 178 × 120 mm in overall envelope with an aspect ratio of approximately 2.97. The central and largest element is the grounded pedestal body (part 8d02bd4c, 1 instance), an axisymmetric revolved solid measuring approximately 60 × 60 × 130 mm with a flared base, tapered body section bearing embossed alphanumeric characters, a bulging shoulder, and a concave neck leading to a flat top disk; its 178 B-spline surfaces, 22 conical faces, 3 toroidal fillets, and 77 planar faces account for the bulk of the assembly's geometric complexity. Seated atop the pedestal is a serrated crown ring (part 8d0a8592, 1 instance) with a pronounced zigzag profile, 62 planar tooth facets, 6 cylindrical inner-bore segments at radius 18.44 mm, and a central through-hole of 36.88 mm diameter; it participates in 4 plane-on-plane contacts with the body. Arrayed around the crown's tips are 10 identical solid spheres of 3.0 mm radius (part 8d0afae4), arranged in a 10-fold rotational pattern about the vertical axis. The two large swept handles are a mirrored pair of curved arms (part 8d0d44b0, 2 instances), each approximately 7.5 × 45.3 × 120 mm with 3 B-spline surfaces defining their S-curved profiles. Two thin profile-cut clips (part 8d0ef264, 2 instances) with C-shaped hook features appear to engage the arms to the body. Two prismatic bar elements—one round-ended parallel key of 7.5 × 7.5 × 120 mm (part 8d0cf680) and one slightly shorter key with a B-spline conforming face (part 8d0ea43e, ~101 mm long)—bridge the handles and the body. Four additional thin, S-curved freeform strips (parts 8d11b16e, 8d135f1c, 8d1497a4, 8d15d01c, 1 instance each), all with aspect ratios around 10–11 and only planar-plus-B-spline surfaces, contribute to the flowing decorative contour of the handles. One part (8d0a375c) has no extracted geometry. Contact edges total 9: 6 mating planes, 2 chamfer contacts, and 1 freeform contact; there are no hole-based fastener connections. The layout tags confirm the 10-fold rotational symmetry of the spheres and 2 mirror-pair groupings among the handle components.

Parts:
- pedestal_body: A solid, largely axisymmetric revolved body approximately 60 × 60 × 130 mm (aspect ratio ~2.17), featuring a flat circular cap at the top, a concave neck, a bulging shoulder, a tapered body with raised embossed text characters, and a flared pedestal base with rounded molding transitions. Its 178 B-spline surfaces and 22 conical faces define the complex compound-curved profile, while 77 planar faces arise mainly from the embossed text. It has no holes; a single plane-on-plane contact at the flat bottom base indicates it sits on a flat surface. This grounded part likely serves as a freestanding pedestal or standing decorative body.
- part: 
- serrated_ring: A thin-walled annular ring (bbox ~50 × 49 × 20 mm, aspect ratio 2.5) with a pronounced periodic zigzag or crown-like profile featuring deep V-shaped notches between pointed teeth around the circumference. A central through-bore of diameter ~36.9 mm runs along the axial direction, formed by six interrupted cylindrical segments at radius 18.44 mm. The 62 planar faces define the angled tooth flanks, and the part participates in 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts. This ring likely serves as a serrated or toothed element that could provide anti-rotation locking or indexing around a cylindrical mating feature.
- bearing_ball (×10): A perfect solid sphere of 3.0 mm radius (6.0 mm diameter, aspect ratio 1.0), composed of a single spherical face with no holes, flats, or secondary features. Ten instances exist in the assembly, and at least one is grounded. The complete absence of planar or cylindrical modifications, combined with the high instance count, suggests this part likely serves as a bearing ball or rolling element that seats into concave races or grooves.
- parallel_key: A slender rectangular bar measuring 7.5 × 7.5 × 120.0 mm (aspect ratio 16:1) with a square cross-section and rounded (bullnose) ends formed by two half-cylindrical faces of radius 5.0 mm. The body has 6 planar faces covering the top, bottom, and long sides plus flat strips at each end, and no holes of any kind. In the assembly it participates in 2 Cone/Plane contact pairs, likely representing engagement with chamfered keyway edges. Based on its proportions, rounded ends, and mating contacts, this part appears to be a parallel key that could transmit torque between a shaft and a hub.
- curved_arm (×2): A long, gently curved strip approximately 120 mm in length with an aspect ratio of about 16, featuring three freeform (B-spline) surfaces forming its curved body and a cylindrical face (radius 5.0 mm) forming a rounded tip at the tapered end. It has four planar faces at the squared-off head and no holes. It contacts a neighboring body via a single Nurbs-on-Nurbs interface and appears in two instances in the assembly. The part likely serves as a curved arm or guide element that mates along its freeform surface with an adjacent curved body.
- key: A long, slender rectangular bar approximately 7.5 × 7.5 × 101 mm with an aspect ratio of about 13.5. Five of its six faces are planar, while one face is a subtle B-spline surface that could conform to a curved mating surface such as a shaft keyway floor. The part has no holes or cylindrical features and contacts neighboring bodies through one plane-on-plane pair and one Nurbs-on-Nurbs pair. Based on its near-square prismatic cross-section, elongated form, and the single curved contact face, this part likely serves as a parallel key that transmits torque between a shaft and a hub.
- clip (×2): A thin, profile-extruded part approximately 2 mm thick with in-plane extents of roughly 24 × 27 mm (aspect ratio ≈ 13.7). Its freeform outline features two open C-shaped hook sections and small angled tab projections, composed of 12 planar and 10 B-spline surfaces with no holes or cylindrical features. Two instances appear in the assembly. The hooks could grip a cylindrical rod, while the tabs could snap into slots, suggesting this part likely serves as a spring or retaining clip.
- curved_support_strip: A very thin (≈1 mm thick), S-curved strip roughly 128 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 10.13. Its geometry comprises two nearly-parallel flat faces connected by three freeform B-spline side surfaces that trace its sinuous outline, with a flared notch at one end and a tapered tip at the other. It has no holes, bosses, or cylindrical features, and participates in a single plane-on-plane contact in the assembly. The part likely serves as a curved structural rib or brace that mates via one flat face to an adjacent body.
- curved_arm: A thin, elongated, S-curved strip approximately 128 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 10.9:1, comprising two nearly-parallel planar faces and three freeform B-spline surfaces that define its organic, gently sinuous perimeter. The part has no holes, no cylindrical features, and no recorded assembly contact pairs, suggesting it could connect to adjacent components via non-bolted means. Its sculptured profile with a wider, knob-like upper end and a flatter, tapered lower tip appears to likely serve as a curved structural arm or profiled support element.
- shaped_leg: A slender, organically curved solid roughly 128 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 10.13, bounded by two parallel flat faces and three freeform B-spline perimeter walls. The profile widens into a lobed end at one extremity and tapers to a rounded paddle at the other, following a gentle S-curve in between. No holes, cylindrical features, or assembly contacts are recorded. The part appears to likely function as a shaped leg or decorative structural strut.
- curved_strip: A very thin (≈0.9 mm thick), elongated S-curved strip about 128 mm long with an aspect ratio of roughly 10.9. It has two flat parallel faces and three freeform B-spline edge surfaces defining its curved outline, including a small notch near the wider top end. No holes, cylinders, or recorded contact pairs are present. Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents three bevel gears arranged so their toothed conical faces converge toward a common mesh zone. The smaller gear sits at the upper left with a thin cylindrical pin protruding axially from its hub, while two larger, identical gears occupy the center and right positions, each carrying a thicker cylindrical shaft through its bore. The overall bounding box spans approximately 426.5 × 323.3 × 265.7 mm with an aspect ratio of about 1.60. The assembly comprises 6 instances drawn from 5 unique parts at a flat hierarchy depth of 1, with 3 parts grounded. The two larger bevel gears (part 79b06576, 2 instances) are the only repeated part and form a mirror pair confirmed by the layout tag. Each hosts a round through-hole of 67.748 mm diameter and 10 mm bore length; one receives a 33.87 mm-radius pin 175 mm long (79b03e80), and the other receives a 33.87 mm-radius pin 100 mm long (79af53d4), both with zero perpendicular misalignment and axial overlaps of about 10.6 mm, indicating tight coaxial fits. The smaller gear (79afa20a, 1 instance) hosts a 28.8 mm-diameter through-hole of 30 mm length that accepts a 14.4 mm-radius pin 120 mm long (79b1021e), again with perfect coaxial alignment (perp distance 0.0, axial overlap 30.6 mm). In total, face-to-face contacts break down as 4 coaxial cylindrical fits and 1 mating planar interface, plus 3 hole-inferred fastener-through-hole links. There is 1 ambiguous contact edge, likely at the tooth-mesh zone where conical gear surfaces interact. The surface-type histogram of 312 planar faces and 146 cylindrical faces reflects the many flat tooth flanks and the cylindrical bores and shafts.

Viewed from a slight isometric angle in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented grapple with a tapered central hub and four evenly spaced curved jaw arms fanning outward and downward; short cylindrical barrels and slender rods bridge between the hub and the mid-sections of each jaw. The single grounded central body (ae62e424, 1 instance) is a bell-shaped end fitting with a lower cylinder of radius 13.5 mm, a conical transition, an upper cylinder of radius 9.39 mm, and an eye lug bearing a 5 mm through-bore at the crown. Wrapping around the lower cylindrical section are eight thin mounting lugs (ae615d7e, 8 instances, aspect ratio 5.77) each carrying a 2.8 mm through-hole for pin retention; four D-shaped lug tabs (ae59bc4c, 4 instances) and four additional mounting lugs (ae6184a4, 4 instances) similarly interface with the upper cylinder, carrying 1.28 mm and 2.48 mm through-holes respectively. The jaw arms come in two mirrored variants — gripper tines (ae4cc3e8, 2 instances) and gripper jaws (ae7c8674, 2 instances) — each roughly 73.6 mm long with B-spline-curved profiles, longitudinal stiffening ribs, and two orthogonally oriented 3.6 mm pin holes at the pivot and mid-body positions. Four hollow cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a, 4 instances, ~28 mm long, 0.7 mm wall, domed end) span between the hub and jaw pivot zones; four follower rods (ae61f99c, 4 instances) with mushroom-head caps and transverse 1.2 mm bored bosses provide the linkage connection. Fasteners comprise four stepped pins (ae594702, shank dia 2.7 mm, head dia 3.6 mm), four flanged pins (ae596e1c, shaft dia 1.2 mm), four spool pins (ae610f34, shaft dia 1.2 mm), and four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a, dia ~1.08 mm). All 26 hole-link inferences show near-zero perpendicular distance (≤ 0.016 mm), confirming tight coaxial alignment. Layout analysis identifies 4-fold rotational symmetry about the Y-axis for eight different part classes and 4 mirror pairs corresponding to the two jaw variants and their associated curved support arms (ae44d4ac, ae6666ac, 2 instances each).

Parts:
- curved_support_arm (×2): A thin, elongated strip roughly 73.6 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 8.65, sweeping through a gentle S- or C-curve defined by freeform (B-spline) surfaces on its broad faces and flat planar side walls. A small tab protrudes near one end, likely serving as a locating or retention interface. With no holes, two instances in the assembly, and edge fillets at R ≈ 1.0–2.1 mm along the edges, this part appears to function as a curved structural support arm or rib, possibly arranged as a symmetric pair.
- gripper_tine (×2): A curved, tapered arm approximately 73.6 mm long with an aspect ratio of 4.32, featuring longitudinal ribs and freeform B-spline surfaces along its length. An eye lug at the top carries a 3.6 mm through-hole oriented along one axis, while a mid-body tab carries a second 3.6 mm through-hole oriented orthogonally, both likely accepting pins for pivoting or linkage connections. The part exists in two instances and tapers to a narrow pointed tip. Given the paired occurrence, dual pivot-capable holes, ribbed stiffening, and claw-like profile, this part appears to function as a tine or jaw in a gripping or clamping mechanism.
- stepped_pin (×4): A small solid stepped cylindrical pin roughly 8.4 mm long and 3.6 mm in maximum diameter (aspect ratio ≈ 2.33). It features three coaxial cylindrical sections—a long main shank (R 1.35 mm, ≈ 7.2 mm long), a short intermediate shoulder (R 1.55 mm, ≈ 0.4 mm), and a wider head (R 1.8 mm, ≈ 0.4 mm)—connected by four toroidal fillet blends, with flat end and annular step faces. With no holes and four instances in the assembly, it likely serves as a shoulder pin or dowel that locates or retains mating components via its stepped diameter transitions.
- flanged_pin (×4): An axially symmetric, spool-shaped solid roughly 8.4 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 2.33, featuring a central shaft of radius 0.6 mm flanked by two disc flanges of different sizes — a large head at radius 1.8 mm and a smaller shoulder at radius 0.95 mm, both approximately 0.41 mm thick. Four toroidal blend surfaces create smooth fillet transitions at each flange junction. With no through-holes, four instances in the assembly, and flat end faces normal to the part axis, this part likely serves as a headed pin or tappet-like locating element whose large flat face could provide a mating or bearing surface while the shaft seats into a receiving bore.
- lug_tab (×4): A thin, D-shaped plate (bbox approximately 1.47 × 5.70 × 9.01 mm, aspect ratio 6.13) with one straight end and one semicircular end (outer radius 9.39 mm). A small through-hole of diameter approximately 1.28 mm passes through the 1.47 mm thickness near the rounded end, surrounded by a concentric cylindrical feature of radius 2.85 mm. Two large opposing flat faces provide potential mating surfaces, while the through-hole could receive a pin or small fastener. Instanced four times in the assembly, this part likely serves as a mounting lug or pivot tab.
- cylindrical_pin_with_retaining_features (×4): An elongated hollow cylindrical body approximately 24 mm long and 6.76 mm in outer diameter (aspect ratio 4.19), capped at one end by a smooth dome formed from B-spline surfaces and open or stepped at the other end. Two small radial bosses protrude from the cylinder wall, each pierced by a through-hole of diameter ~0.864 mm that could accept a retaining pin or clip. Longitudinal flat cuts (indicated by opposing plane normals perpendicular to the main axis) appear to provide anti-rotation capability. Viewed from a slight isometric angle in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented grapple with a tapered central hub and four evenly spaced curved jaw arms fanning outward and downward; short cylindrical barrels and slender rods bridge between the hub and the mid-sections of each jaw. The single grounded central body (ae62e424, 1 instance) is a bell-shaped end fitting with a lower cylinder of radius 13.5 mm, a conical transition, an upper cylinder of radius 9.39 mm, and an eye lug bearing a 5 mm through-bore at the crown. Wrapping around the lower cylindrical section are eight thin mounting lugs (ae615d7e, 8 instances, aspect ratio 5.77) each carrying a 2.8 mm through-hole for pin retention; four D-shaped lug tabs (ae59bc4c, 4 instances) and four additional mounting lugs (ae6184a4, 4 instances) similarly interface with the upper cylinder, carrying 1.28 mm and 2.48 mm through-holes respectively. The jaw arms come in two mirrored variants — gripper tines (ae4cc3e8, 2 instances) and gripper jaws (ae7c8674, 2 instances) — each roughly 73.6 mm long with B-spline-curved profiles, longitudinal stiffening ribs, and two orthogonally oriented 3.6 mm pin holes at the pivot and mid-body positions. Four hollow cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a, 4 instances, ~28 mm long, 0.7 mm wall, domed end) span between the hub and jaw pivot zones; four follower rods (ae61f99c, 4 instances) with mushroom-head caps and transverse 1.2 mm bored bosses provide the linkage connection. Fasteners comprise four stepped pins (ae594702, shank dia 2.7 mm, head dia 3.6 mm), four flanged pins (ae596e1c, shaft dia 1.2 mm), four spool pins (ae610f34, shaft dia 1.2 mm), and four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a, dia ~1.08 mm). All 26 hole-link inferences show near-zero perpendicular distance (≤ 0.016 mm), confirming tight coaxial alignment. Layout analysis identifies 4-fold rotational symmetry about the Y-axis for eight different part classes and 4 mirror pairs corresponding to the two jaw variants and their associated curved support arms (ae44d4ac, ae6666ac, 2 instances each).

Present in four grounded instances with no recorded contact pairs, this part appears to serve as a mounting lug or hinge ear whose through-hole could accept a pin or small fastener while the flat rectangular end likely attaches rigidly to a supporting structure.
- cylindrical_pin (×4): A solid, featureless cylinder with a single outer radius of 0.5382 and an axial extent of approximately 1.084, yielding a nearly equal diameter-to-height proportion (aspect ratio 1.378). - follower_rod (×4): A slender elongated body (aspect ratio ~4.4, bbox ~5 × 15.5 × 22.3 mm) consisting of a thin mushroom-head disc at one end (radius ~2.53 mm), a long cylindrical stem (radius ~0.54 mm, ~16.6 mm long), and a transverse cylindrical boss at the opposite end containing a through-hole of diameter 1.20 mm. - end_fitting_with_eye: A bell-shaped solid body approximately 27 × 27 × 43.5 mm (aspect ratio ~1.61) featuring a wide cylindrical base (R 13.5 mm), a conical taper to a narrower upper cylinder (R 9.39 mm), and a flat protruding lug at the top with a rounded profile (R ~5.07 mm) containing a 5 mm-diameter through-bore (3 mm deep). Eight planar faces with normals in all six cardinal directions reflect the rectangular lug atop the axially-symmetric body. - curved_arm (×2): A long, slender, gently curved solid body approximately 73.6 mm in length with an aspect ratio of 8.648, featuring broad B-spline-surfaced flanks, flat planar side walls, and a beveled lower end. At the upper tip, a small cylindrical protrusion of R = 1.0 mm extends about 11 mm, terminating in a rounded dome and blended into the main body with fillet transitions. - gripper_jaw (×2): An elongated, gently curved arm approximately 73.6 mm long with an aspect ratio of 4.32, tapering from a ribbed mid-section to a narrow tip. Annotation Caveats:
The contact_edge_counts dictionary is empty, so all inter-part connectivity is inferred solely from the 26 hole-link edges; no conventional planar or cylindrical mating contacts were recorded. Several large-diameter hole-link clusters (27 mm and 18.78 mm) actually represent coaxial cylindrical wrap-fits between lug outer profiles and the central body's cylindrical sections, not true bores — the hole detector misinterprets these curved perimeter surfaces as through-holes. The 5 mm bore in the eye lug of the end fitting (ae62e424) and the ~0.86 mm radial holes on the cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a) have no matching fastener in the hole_edges_summary, meaning either no fastener body is modeled or the match fell outside detection thresholds. The four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a) likewise appear in no hole-link cluster. All 49 instances are marked grounded, preventing any kinematic inference from the assembly tree; the flat tree depth of 1 indicates no sub-assembly grouping.

Viewed from an isometric angle, the assembly presents as a shallow rectangular console (bbox 309 × 65 × 247 mm, aspect ratio ~4.75) with its major extent along the X-axis. The single grounded housing cover (300 × 230 × 45 mm, 157 planar + 126 cylindrical faces) forms the visible top deck containing arrays of rectangular cutouts with 1.0 mm corner fillets, into which 26 spacer-block instances (18.87 × 12.09 × 5.0 mm each, filleted-top rectangular prisms) are seated via plane-on-plane and cylinder-on-plane contacts, forming the raised tactile pads visible in groups across the surface. Beneath sits the base plate (309 × 245 × 28 mm, aspect ratio 11.1), a thin slab with elongated surface grooves and stepped edges that mates to the underside of the cover through 17 planar contacts. Two large disc-shaped caps (86 mm diameter, 20 mm tall), each bearing 14 circumferential slots and a small hemispherical indicator boss, sit in circular recesses and represent the jog-wheel elements—they connect via 2 plane-on-plane contacts each. Eleven solid cylindrical pins (diameter 1.5 mm, length 11 mm) protrude vertically through the top surface as control stems; no contact edges are recorded for these, suggesting press-fit or loose insertion into the socket posts. Eight bell-shaped socket posts (14.89 mm base diameter, 17 mm tall, with 1.178 mm through-holes) are distributed across the panel as receptacles. Six flanged bushings (flange diameter ~18.2 mm, central bore ~4.92 mm) likely serve as the front-panel jack connectors visible in the render. Three wedge guide blocks and three structural bars are arranged in 3-fold rotational patterns about axes perpendicular to the panel plane, likely forming a central mixer-crossfader region. Two ball studs (spherical head diameter ~18.2 mm) and three locating pins (mushroom-headed, with perpendicular cross-holes) complete the assembly. Layout tags confirm: overall planar arrangement (normal along Y), two 3-fold rotational clusters, and 2 mirror pairs reflecting the left-right symmetry of the dual-deck layout.

- flanged_bushing (×6): A small flanged body approximately 10 mm deep with a ~18.2 mm diameter flange and a cylindrical boss (radius ~5.03 mm, 5 mm long) on one side, plus a central bore of radius ~2.46 mm extending 8 mm axially. Annotation Caveats:
No fastener-through-hole stacks were detected (hole_edge_count = 0), so all retention between parts is inferred as press-fit, snap, or adhesive. Viewed in the render, the assembly presents a wedge-shaped silhouette: a bulbous, multi-faceted cap on the left transitions into a thin, flat fin plate that tapers to a sharp point on the right. Five circular openings are arranged in a line along the top ridge of the fin, a concave arc cutout sweeps along one side to clear the hub cap, and two small wire rings protrude from the lower face and the pointed tip. The single-instance fin plate (part 98bbd162, role 'fin_plate') is the largest component, with approximate extents of 21.85 × 11.28 × 1.5 mm, an aspect ratio of 14.57, and a surface inventory of 34 conical faces (45° chamfer strips and conical bore profiles), 20 planar faces, 17 cylindrical faces, and 2 B-spline surfaces forming the freeform outer contour. Its holes_on_part list is empty because the openings carry conical chamfer transitions rather than pure cylindrical bores. Two further part classes each appear in 2 instances and form the 2 detected mirror pairs: one pair likely constitutes the two half-shells of the rounded hub cap visible at the blunt end, and the other pair corresponds to the two small toroidal rings. A fourth unique part (1 instance) may be the inner cylindrical boss or bearing seat visible at the hub centre. All 6 instances are grounded. Contact evidence totals 10 face-to-face contacts — 4 mating-plane interfaces (flat-face abutments, principally the fin plate to hub shells), 3 coaxial cylindrical fits (bore-to-bore or bore-to-shaft alignments at the hub and openings), and 3 freeform contacts (chamfered cone surfaces meeting B-spline shells). The surface-type histogram across the full assembly — Cylinder 44, Plane 41, Cone 36, BSplineSurface 34, Torus 12 — reflects the extensive chamfering, the freeform shell curvatures, and the toroidal blends or ring features. No fastener-through-hole stacks are inferred; the five openings in the fin remain unoccupied.

Viewed in the render, the assembly reads as a stylised chair: a tall pointed backrest panel rises roughly 215 mm above a broader seat platform, while four waisted legs descend about 100 mm below. The overall bounding box measures 110 × 300 × 100 mm with an aspect ratio of 3.0 along the tallest axis, and the total volume is approximately 440 384 mm³ distributed across 159 faces (108 planar, 49 cylindrical, 2 B-spline), 381 edges, and 232 vertices. The tree depth is 1 (flat hierarchy, no sub-assemblies), and none of the 5 instances is grounded.

The single decorative bracket (part 3b67f32e, 1 instance) is the largest body, measuring roughly 100 × 110 × 215 mm. Its vertical plate carries a pointed-leaf contour defined by the two B-spline surfaces, with several diamond-shaped through-cutouts whose wall normals are tilted approximately 30° from the primary axes. The horizontal base includes semicircular arc features (R ≈ 50 mm) and additional curved contours (R ≈ 20 mm, R ≈ 10 mm). Four leg parts — two mirrored column_spacers (part 3b66e190, 2 instances), one column (part 3b677db8, 1 instance), and one column_spacer (part 3b666c40, 1 instance) — each occupy a 25 × 25 × 100 mm envelope with an aspect ratio of 4.0. Their side faces are large-radius concave cylinders (R ≈ 130–145 mm) producing the distinctive waisted silhouette, with R = 2 mm fillet rounds at vertical edges. All 16 contact edges in the assembly are mating-plane pairs; no hole-based fastener connections exist. The layout_tags report one mirror_pair, corresponding to the two identical column_spacer instances symmetrically placed beneath the seat.

Small 2.0 mm-radius fillets round the vertical corners, and angled planar chamfers flare the cross-section at each end. The part has no holes and participates in 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts through its flat end and chamfer faces. It likely serves as a structural column or post element that mates between flat surfaces.
- column_spacer: A 25 × 25 × 100 mm solid column (aspect ratio 4.0) with a square footprint whose four sides curve concavely inward via large-radius cylindrical surfaces (R ≈ 130–145 mm), creating a waisted or hourglass profile. Small R = 2 mm fillets run along vertical edges between the concave panels. The top and bottom faces are flat planes, and the part participates in 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts, indicating it likely functions as a spacer or standoff post that mates with flat surfaces at each end.
- column_spacer (×2): A 25 × 25 × 100 mm solid column (aspect ratio 4.0) with a square footprint and four gently concave longitudinal sides formed by large-radius cylindrical surfaces (R ≈ 130–145 mm), giving it a waisted profile that narrows toward the mid-height. Small 2 mm-radius fillets round the four vertical edges, while flat planar caps at top and bottom provide mating faces. With 8 Plane/Plane contact pairs in the assembly and two instances present, the part appears to serve as a paired structural spacer or standoff column sandwiched between flat receiving surfaces.
- decorative_bracket: An L-shaped bracket-like solid approximately 100 × 110 × 215 mm (aspect ratio 2.15), consisting of a thin vertical plate rising from a horizontal base, with a fill ratio of only about 10 % of its bounding box. The vertical plate carries a pointed-leaf B-spline contour and several diamond-shaped through-cutouts, while the base features semicircular arcs (R ≈ 50 mm) on its sides and additional diamond cutouts on its top face. The part mates with neighbors through 16 plane-on-plane contacts across its multiple flat faces and has no cylindrical holes. It likely serves as a structural or decorative bracket, with the diamond cutouts possibly permitting tab engagement or serving as weight-reduction features.

The single decorative bracket (part 3b67f32e, 1 instance) is the largest body, measuring roughly 100 × 110 × 215 mm. Its vertical plate carries a pointed-leaf contour defined by the two B-spline surfaces, with several diamond-shaped through-cutouts whose wall normals are tilted approximately 30° from the primary axes. The horizontal base includes semicircular arc features (R ≈ 50 mm) and additional curved contours (R ≈ 20 mm, R ≈ 10 mm). Four leg parts — two mirrored column_spacers (part 3b66e190, 2 instances), one column (part 3b677db8, 1 instance), and one column_spacer (part 3b666c40, 1 instance) — each occupy a 25 × 25 × 100 mm envelope with an aspect ratio of 4.0. Their side faces are large-radius concave cylinders (R ≈ 130–145 mm) producing the distinctive waisted silhouette, with R = 2 mm fillet rounds at vertical edges. All 16 contact edges in the assembly are mating-plane pairs; no hole-based fastener connections exist. The layout_tags report one mirror_pair, corresponding to the two identical column_spacer instances symmetrically placed beneath the seat.

In the rendered image the assembly stands vertically, roughly 800 mm tall and 277 mm across, with a flat disc plate at the top, a long thin rod descending from it, and a barrel-shaped cage of slats at the lower end. The grounded part is the disc plate (180 mm diameter × 10 mm thick, one central 10 mm through-hole), which serves as the topmost anchor. The rod is a featureless solid cylinder of 10 mm diameter and 500 mm length (aspect ratio 50) that passes through the disc plate's central bore; hole inference confirms this link with zero perpendicular misalignment and 0.3 mm axial overlap. At the lower end of the rod, a flanged bushing (56.5 mm outer flange diameter, 40 mm through-bore, 11.25 mm tall) and a bushing/locating plug (20 mm main-body radius, 23 mm rim radius, 77 mm tall with a conical lower taper and a blind 24 mm bore at the top) are linked coaxially through a second hole inference edge (diameter 40 mm, zero perpendicular misalignment, 11.85 mm axial overlap matching the bushing's full height). These hub components sit at the top of the cage. A splined ring (200 mm diameter, 3.9 mm thick, 40 external teeth, inner bore ~160 mm) encircles the hub zone; its 40 teeth match the 40 vane slats in count. Each vane slat is an extremely thin panel (~3.9 × 50 × 300 mm, aspect ratio 76.9) with a gentle outward curvature (cylinder radius 572.5 mm on one face) and two edge notches that likely engage with the splined ring's teeth for location and retention. A layout tag confirms a 40-fold rotational arrangement of the slats about the Y-axis, centered at approximately y = −80.4 mm. Completing the assembly is a knob (60 mm wide, 81 mm tall) with a spherical upper dome and conical stem that appears to cap the hub. One part (3f1c184a, single instance) lacks geometric data and may serve as an additional connector or spacer in the hub region.

With no holes, no recorded contact pairs, and an instance count of 40 in the assembly, this part appears to function as a repeated vane or slat element that could slot into a surrounding frame structure via its notches.

The blind bore could receive a shaft or pin, and the tapered end appears to facilitate guided insertion or press-fitting. This part likely functions as a locating bushing or guide plug.
- flanged_bushing: An axisymmetric flanged ring measuring 56.5 mm in outer diameter and 11.25 mm tall (aspect ratio ~5). A thin flange at the base (3.25 mm thick, 56.5 mm OD) steps up to a narrower cylindrical boss (44.25 mm OD, 8.0 mm tall) with a 40 mm-diameter central through-bore spanning the full height. The flat flange bottom and the boss outer surface appear to serve as mating interfaces — the flange likely seats against a flat surface while the boss could locate into a matching bore. This part likely functions as a flanged bushing or collar.
- knob: A solid axially symmetric body with a spherical top (roughly 30 mm radius) blending into a conical taper that ends at a small flat circular base, fitting within a 60 × 60 × 81 mm bounding box (aspect ratio 1.35). The geometry is extremely simple — only three faces (sphere, cone, plane) with no holes or cylindrical features. The sole flat base face (normal [0, −1, 0]) is the only candidate mating surface; no through-holes are present. This part likely serves as a knob or grip element, with the spherical portion appearing sized for hand contact and the conical stem potentially seating into a receiving socket.
- vane_slat (×40): This part is a very thin, elongated panel approximately 3.9 × 50 × 300 mm (aspect ratio ~77) with one gently curved broad face defined by a 572.5 mm radius cylindrical surface, producing a subtle convex bow. Two small notches with filleted corners (radii 15 mm and 5 mm) are located near the top and bottom of one straight edge, likely serving as locating or retention features. With no holes, no recorded contact pairs, and an instance count of 40 in the assembly, this part appears to function as a repeated vane or slat element that could slot into a surrounding frame structure via its notches.
- part: 
- splined_ring: A thin annular ring approximately 200 mm in outer diameter and only 3.9 mm thick (aspect ratio ~51.3), featuring roughly 40 evenly-spaced rectangular teeth around its outer edge at radius 100 mm and a smooth central through-bore of diameter ~160 mm. The two flat annular faces permit axial stacking, while the external teeth could engage a mating internally-toothed bore. No assembly contact pairs are recorded. This part appears likely to function as a splined ring or separator plate that could transmit torque through its external spline teeth.
- rod: A featureless solid cylinder 500 mm long with a 10 mm diameter (radius 5.0 mm), giving an aspect ratio of 50. It comprises a single cylindrical surface, two flat circular end caps, and no holes, steps, or other features. No contact pairs are recorded in the assembly. Its extreme slenderness and uniform cross-section suggest it likely serves as a plain rod, pin, or shaft.
- disc_plate: A flat circular disc 180 mm in diameter and 10 mm thick (aspect ratio 18.0) with a single central through-hole of 10 mm diameter penetrating its full thickness. The top and bottom faces are broad, parallel planes, and the outer rim is a smooth cylindrical surface at 90 mm radius. No assembly contact pairs are recorded, but the part is grounded. The central bore could permit a shaft or fastener to pass through, and the large flat faces appear to serve as potential mating surfaces, suggesting this part likely functions as a base disc or cover plate.

Annotation Caveats:
Part 3f1c184a (1 instance) has null extraction status and no geometric description, so its role is unknown. Contact-edge counts are entirely empty; no face-to-face contacts were recorded in the assembly graph, meaning all inter-part relationships are inferred from hole edges and geometric correspondence alone. The rod-to-disc-plate hole link shows only 0.3 mm axial overlap relative to a 10 mm hole length, which may indicate the rod is nearly flush or the overlap detection is marginal. The matched_radius_mean of 23 mm in the bushing-to-plug hole edge exceeds the bore's nominal 20 mm radius, suggesting the plug's 23 mm rim shoulder was matched rather than the main body cylinder.

In the rendered image the assembly stands vertically, roughly 800 mm tall and 277 mm across, with a flat disc plate at the top, a long thin rod descending from it, and a barrel-shaped cage of slats at the lower end. The grounded part is the disc plate (180 mm diameter × 10 mm thick, one central 10 mm through-hole), which serves as the topmost anchor. The rod is a featureless solid cylinder of 10 mm diameter and 500 mm length (aspect ratio 50) that passes through the disc plate's central bore; hole inference confirms this link with zero perpendicular misalignment and 0.3 mm axial overlap. At the lower end of the rod, a flanged bushing (56.5 mm outer flange diameter, 40 mm through-bore, 11.25 mm tall) and a bushing/locating plug (20 mm main-body radius, 23 mm rim radius, 77 mm tall with a conical lower taper and a blind 24 mm bore at the top) are linked coaxially through a second hole inference edge (diameter 40 mm, zero perpendicular misalignment, 11.85 mm axial overlap matching the bushing's full height). These hub components sit at the top of the cage. A splined ring (200 mm diameter, 3.9 mm thick, 40 external teeth, inner bore ~160 mm) encircles the hub zone; its 40 teeth match the 40 vane slats in count. Each vane slat is an extremely thin panel (~3.9 × 50 × 300 mm, aspect ratio 76.9) with a gentle outward curvature (cylinder radius 572.5 mm on one face) and two edge notches that likely engage with the splined ring's teeth for location and retention. A layout tag confirms a 40-fold rotational arrangement of the slats about the Y-axis, centered at approximately y = −80.4 mm. Completing the assembly is a knob (60 mm wide, 81 mm tall) with a spherical upper dome and conical stem that appears to cap the hub. One part (3f1c184a, single instance) lacks geometric data and may serve as an additional connector or spacer in the hub region.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents an elongated warship figurine whose hull tapers symmetrically fore and aft, with the longest dimension running approximately 100 mm. Three squat cylindrical bosses (radius 5.0 mm each) rise from the deck as turret bases, two flanking the central superstructure and one farther forward or aft; a smaller cylinder (radius 2.5 mm) projects upward from the superstructure as a chimney-like element. All 11 instances are grounded, and the assembly tree is flat (depth 1) with no nested sub-assemblies.

The hull body (part c502ddfe, 1 instance) accounts for the vast majority of the assembly's 75 faces, carrying 39 planar faces (deck, base, superstructure walls, and angled chamfer transitions at ±45°), 4 cylindrical faces, and 1 BSplineSurface that wraps the hull's lower contour, producing 3 elliptical transition edges. Nine pin parts (c5037a46) are solid cylinders of diameter 1.0 and length 10.0, each with just 3 faces; these correspond to the triple gun-barrel clusters visible at each turret. A single spacer part (c5054f40) is a partial-cylindrical segment (bbox ~2.1 × 6.2 × 9.7, 4 faces including 1 BSpline surface and 2 oblique planar end caps with elliptical edges) that mates to the hull via 2 of the assembly's 2 total mating-plane contacts. The layout is tagged as a linear chain along the Z-axis (the ship's length), consistent with the fore-to-aft arrangement of turrets and superstructure. No hole edges or fastener-through-hole stacks were detected; the pins appear to be placed into the turret bosses without recorded cylindrical contact pairs.

Viewed from above and slightly to one side, the assembly presents as a low-profile, organic, pebble-shaped enclosure with a visible circumferential parting line dividing an upper and a lower dome, and a thin disc-like element visible at the seam. The layout is tagged as a linear chain along the Y-axis, reflecting the vertical stacking of the three unique, single-instance parts. The base cover (part 4607dfae) is the largest body at roughly 303.9 × 159.5 × 72.0 mm with an aspect ratio of 4.22; its geometry comprises 9 B-spline surfaces forming the curved walls and 31 planar faces for the flat base and small embossed top-deck details, plus 4 cylindrical faces at a radius of 6.0 mm with an 8.0 mm axial extent that correspond to a small circular boss or recess on the top deck. The housing cover (part 461c9fae) is somewhat smaller at 227.4 × 119.7 × 50.0 mm (aspect ratio 4.55) and is overwhelmingly freeform, with 292 B-spline faces and 19 planar faces; its upper surface features an oval recessed panel containing five small circular-edge dot features and several embossed relief symbols arranged in a cross pattern, clearly visible in the render. The cover plate (part 468e7438) is an extremely thin oval disc at 241.82 × 126.86 × 6.0 mm (aspect ratio ~40:1) with only 3 faces — two opposing planar faces and one B-spline rim — and no holes, bosses, or other features. No contact edges of any surface-pair type were recorded in the assembly graph, and no hole-link inferences exist because none of the three parts carry any through-holes or blind holes. All three parts are individually grounded. The absence of any detected mating interfaces suggests the parts nest concentrically by geometry alone, possibly retained by snap-fit detents or adhesive bonds that are not modeled as contact surfaces in the CAD data.
