The rendered image shows an articulated arm in a raised-elbow pose, extending from a wide circular base at the lower left up and to the right where a two-jaw gripper is visible in an open configuration. The overall bounding box spans 830 × 732 × 403 mm with an aspect ratio of 2.06. The single grounded part is the housing cap (56d3daba), a disc-like body approximately 390.5 × 390.5 × 104 mm with a stepped cylindrical profile (outer radius 150 mm, inner ledge at 130 mm radius), a central locating boss at 15 mm radius, and an array of peripheral mounting lugs whose radially-oriented flat faces produce numerous plane normals at 30° intervals. Attached above this base is the lever arm with bevel gear (56d86e68, 180 × 228 × 323 mm), featuring concentric cylindrical steps at 125, 90, and 68.5 mm radius forming the turntable interface, and 24 radially arranged tooth features at its upper hub suggesting a bevel gear mesh. The connecting arm (56b8152e, 442.5 × 120 × 62 mm, aspect ratio 7.14) is the long curved link visible in the image, with hub bores at each end (radius 15 mm) and a bolted flange carrying 13 blind holes of diameter 6 mm. The joint housing (56e65190, 93.9 × 132.5 × 296 mm) provides the elbow joint with its L-shaped geometry, two perpendicular blind bores of 30 mm diameter, a 44 mm through-bore, and a 12-hole bolt-circle flange. The multi-bore housing (56e42e6e, 76.2 × 80 × 173.1 mm) carries the characteristic multi-lobed profile visible in the forearm, with a large cross-bore at 85 mm radius and longitudinal bores at 35.4 mm radius. The scroll housing (56e3b934, 87.6 × 70 × 55.4 mm) sits at the wrist with a circular rim of 35 mm radius and four through-holes for mounting. Two gripper jaws (56d365ba, 19.5 × 70.9 × 126.9 mm each) form a mirror pair with serrated gripping faces, a pivot boss at radius 15 mm, and elongated adjustment slots. Twelve spacer rings (56de3b2e, 6 × 6 × 3 mm each, bore diameter 3.2 mm) are arranged in a 12-fold rotational pattern whose axis aligns approximately with the base's vertical, likely functioning as bearing spacers or roller-simulating elements in the turntable joint. Contact-edge counts total 20 mating-plane and 6 coaxial-fit interfaces, with 4 ambiguous contacts, and no fastener-through-hole stacks were inferred (consistent with all bolt holes being blind). Layout tags confirm a roughly planar part arrangement, the 12-fold rotational cluster of spacers, and a single mirror pair corresponding to the two gripper jaws.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented trophy form roughly 60 × 178 × 120 mm in overall envelope with an aspect ratio of approximately 2.97. The central and largest element is the grounded pedestal body (part 8d02bd4c, 1 instance), an axisymmetric revolved solid measuring approximately 60 × 60 × 130 mm with a flared base, tapered body section bearing embossed alphanumeric characters, a bulging shoulder, and a concave neck leading to a flat top disk; its 178 B-spline surfaces, 22 conical faces, 3 toroidal fillets, and 77 planar faces account for the bulk of the assembly's geometric complexity. Seated atop the pedestal is a serrated crown ring (part 8d0a8592, 1 instance) with a pronounced zigzag profile, 62 planar tooth facets, 6 cylindrical inner-bore segments at radius 18.44 mm, and a central through-hole of 36.88 mm diameter; it participates in 4 plane-on-plane contacts with the body. Arrayed around the crown's tips are 10 identical solid spheres of 3.0 mm radius (part 8d0afae4), arranged in a 10-fold rotational pattern about the vertical axis. The two large swept handles are a mirrored pair of curved arms (part 8d0d44b0, 2 instances), each approximately 7.5 × 45.3 × 120 mm with 3 B-spline surfaces defining their S-curved profiles. Two thin profile-cut clips (part 8d0ef264, 2 instances) with C-shaped hook features appear to engage the arms to the body. Two prismatic bar elements—one round-ended parallel key of 7.5 × 7.5 × 120 mm (part 8d0cf680) and one slightly shorter key with a B-spline conforming face (part 8d0ea43e, ~101 mm long)—bridge the handles and the body. Four additional thin, S-curved freeform strips (parts 8d11b16e, 8d135f1c, 8d1497a4, 8d15d01c, 1 instance each), all with aspect ratios around 10–11 and only planar-plus-B-spline surfaces, contribute to the flowing decorative contour of the handles. One part (8d0a375c) has no extracted geometry. Contact edges total 9: 6 mating planes, 2 chamfer contacts, and 1 freeform contact; there are no hole-based fastener connections. The layout tags confirm the 10-fold rotational symmetry of the spheres and 2 mirror-pair groupings among the handle components.

It has no holes, bosses, or cylindrical features, and participates in a single plane-on-plane contact in the assembly. The part likely serves as a curved structural rib or brace that mates via one flat face to an adjacent body.
- curved_arm: A thin, elongated, S-curved strip approximately 128 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 10.9:1, comprising two nearly-parallel planar faces and three freeform B-spline surfaces that define its organic, gently sinuous perimeter. The part has no holes, no cylindrical features, and no recorded assembly contact pairs, suggesting it could connect to adjacent components via non-bolted means. The profile widens into a lobed end at one extremity and tapers to a rounded paddle at the other, following a gentle S-curve in between. No holes, cylindrical features, or assembly contacts are recorded. The part appears to likely function as a shaped leg or decorative structural strut.
- curved_strip: A very thin (≈0.9 mm thick), elongated S-curved strip about 128 mm long with an aspect ratio of roughly 10.9. The two large swept handles are a mirrored pair of curved arms (part 8d0d44b0, 2 instances), each approximately 7.5 × 45.3 × 120 mm with 3 B-spline surfaces defining their S-curved profiles. Two thin profile-cut clips (part 8d0ef264, 2 instances) with C-shaped hook features appear to engage the arms to the body. Two prismatic bar elements—one round-ended parallel key of 7.5 × 7.5 × 120 mm (part 8d0cf680) and one slightly shorter key with a B-spline conforming face (part 8d0ea43e, ~101 mm long)—bridge the handles and the body. Four additional thin, S-curved freeform strips (parts 8d11b16e, 8d135f1c, 8d1497a4, 8d15d01c, 1 instance each), all with aspect ratios around 10–11 and only planar-plus-B-spline surfaces, contribute to the flowing decorative contour of the handles. One part (8d0a375c) has no extracted geometry. Contact edges total 9: 6 mating planes, 2 chamfer contacts, and 1 freeform contact; there are no hole-based fastener connections. The layout tags confirm the 10-fold rotational symmetry of the spheres and 2 mirror-pair groupings among the handle components.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly forms a roughly triangular skeletal frame spanning approximately 600 × 501 × 527 mm, with an aspect ratio near 1.2. The triangular linkage bracket (c0024a12) is the largest structural member, featuring three arms radiating from a central open web with B-spline-blended transitions, carrying two Ø40 mm × 100 mm through-holes at its upper vertices and one Ø30 mm through-hole at its lower-left vertex. Mating with it is a U-shaped curved link arm (bfd260d0, ~60 × 380 × 410 mm) whose swept B-spline body terminates in two cylindrical bosses, each bored with a Ø40 mm × 100 mm through-hole aligned along the X-axis; a Ø50 mm × 15 mm blind hole on its lateral face serves as a locating recess. Four identical pin/shaft parts (bfd12828), arranged in a 4-fold rotational pattern about an axis near Z, pass through the Ø40 mm bores — two through the link arm and two through the triangular bracket — with perfect coaxial alignment (perpendicular distance 0.0 mm, axial overlap 100.6 mm). Two solid Ø30 mm × 90 mm pins (bfd100ee) form a mirror pair and are grounded, providing fixed pivot axes; a single Ø30 mm × 100 mm pin (bfce1ad2) passes through a host part (bfce4234) at the bracket's lower-left vertex with tight alignment. A pulley disc (bfd21264, ~230 mm outer diameter, 61 mm thick) with a stepped hub, five Ø14 mm bolt-circle through-holes, and periodic small features around its rim is mounted on one face via a planar mating contact — no fastener bodies for its bolt holes are modeled. A grounded flat bar (bfd19d36, 600 × 50 × 10 mm, aspect ratio 60) spans the top of the assembly. The contact graph totals 20 face-to-face contacts: 9 mating planes, 8 coaxial fits, 2 spherical contacts, and 1 cylinder-sphere contact, with 5 fastener-through-hole links inferred from hole geometry.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents a horizontally oriented barrel sitting in a low rectangular cradle. The barrel's body is the dominant structure: 18 identical curved segments (cd83668a, each ~33 × 33.5 × 259.6 mm, aspect ratio 7.875) tile together in an 18-fold rotational pattern about the barrel's long axis (layout tag: n_fold_rotation, n = 18, axis [0, 0, −1]). Each stave has B-spline-dominated curvature and stepped tongue-and-groove end features defined by small-radius cylindrical patches (R ≈ 62.4 mm) and planar faces, which interlock adjacent staves. Encircling the stave assembly are four annular hoop rings in two size classes: two larger rings (~188.5 mm outer diameter, cd8a9240) and two smaller rings (~163 mm outer diameter, cd8a443a), each with sculpted toroidal-conical-B-spline cross-sections and no flat faces. Two disc caps (~141.2 mm diameter, 12 mm thick) close the barrel ends — one plain (cd8a1ce4, conical chamfer and toroidal blend rim) and one decorated (cd8784dc, 44 B-spline surfaces forming embossed script, plus a 10 mm-radius cylindrical locating boss 3 mm deep). A flanged collar (cd8b5590, ~40 mm flange diameter, 15 mm tall, with a countersunk through-hole of 23 mm mouth / 15 mm bore diameter) sits atop the barrel as a bung fitting, capped by a simple solid cylindrical plug (cd8ba3a8, R 16.25 mm, 14 mm tall). The tap sub-assembly on the front face consists of a dual-lobe knob (cd86c1b6, ~30.5 × 32 × 45 mm, blind bore R 4 mm, depth ~27.5 mm), a ball-lever handle (cd870fc2, sphere with a 5 mm-diameter lever arm and a disc base R ≈ 10.6 mm), and a spacer sleeve (cd8736da, outer R 4 mm, inner R 3 mm, 40 mm long, 6 mm through-hole). The cradle comprises two saddle supports (cd7e5d26 and cd7fe3e4, each ~170 × 45 × 25 mm, with concave semicylindrical channels R 12.5 mm forming a 2-member mirror pair per the mirror_pair layout tag), two prismatic crossbars (~25 × 25 × 220 mm each, one structural bar cd814340, one spacer bar cd8206c2 with raised pads), and a trapezoidal-section key bar (cd82ca18, ~25 × 25 × 189 mm, 22° angled sides). No contact edges or hole-edge links were recorded in the assembly graph, so all interface inferences rest on geometric compatibility alone.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents a horizontally oriented barrel sitting in a low rectangular cradle. The barrel's body is the dominant structure: 18 identical curved segments (cd83668a, each ~33 × 33.5 × 259.6 mm, aspect ratio 7.875) tile together in an 18-fold rotational pattern about the barrel's long axis (layout tag: n_fold_rotation, n = 18, axis [0, 0, −1]). Each stave has B-spline-dominated curvature and stepped tongue-and-groove end features defined by small-radius cylindrical patches (R ≈ 62.4 mm) and planar faces, which interlock adjacent staves. Encircling the stave assembly are four annular hoop rings in two size classes: two larger rings (~188.5 mm outer diameter, cd8a9240) and two smaller rings (~163 mm outer diameter, cd8a443a), each with sculpted toroidal-conical-B-spline cross-sections and no flat faces. Two disc caps (~141.2 mm diameter, 12 mm thick) close the barrel ends — one plain (cd8a1ce4, conical chamfer and toroidal blend rim) and one decorated (cd8784dc, 44 B-spline surfaces forming embossed script, plus a 10 mm-radius cylindrical locating boss 3 mm deep). A flanged collar (cd8b5590, ~40 mm flange diameter, 15 mm tall, with a countersunk through-hole of 23 mm mouth / 15 mm bore diameter) sits atop the barrel as a bung fitting, capped by a simple solid cylindrical plug (cd8ba3a8, R 16.25 mm, 14 mm tall). The tap sub-assembly on the front face consists of a dual-lobe knob (cd86c1b6, ~30.5 × 32 × 45 mm, blind bore R 4 mm, depth ~27.5 mm), a ball-lever handle (cd870fc2, sphere with a 5 mm-diameter lever arm and a disc base R ≈ 10.6 mm), and a spacer sleeve (cd8736da, outer R 4 mm, inner R 3 mm, 40 mm long, 6 mm through-hole). The cradle comprises two saddle supports (cd7e5d26 and cd7fe3e4, each ~170 × 45 × 25 mm, with concave semicylindrical channels R 12.5 mm forming a 2-member mirror pair per the mirror_pair layout tag), two prismatic crossbars (~25 × 25 × 220 mm each, one structural bar cd814340, one spacer bar cd8206c2 with raised pads), and a trapezoidal-section key bar (cd82ca18, ~25 × 25 × 189 mm, 22° angled sides). No contact edges or hole-edge links were recorded in the assembly graph, so all interface inferences rest on geometric compatibility alone.

Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents three bevel gears arranged so their toothed conical faces converge toward a common mesh zone. The smaller gear sits at the upper left with a thin cylindrical pin protruding axially from its hub, while two larger, identical gears occupy the center and right positions, each carrying a thicker cylindrical shaft through its bore. The overall bounding box spans approximately 426.5 × 323.3 × 265.7 mm with an aspect ratio of about 1.60. The assembly comprises 6 instances drawn from 5 unique parts at a flat hierarchy depth of 1, with 3 parts grounded. The two larger bevel gears (part 79b06576, 2 instances) are the only repeated part and form a mirror pair confirmed by the layout tag. Each hosts a round through-hole of 67.748 mm diameter and 10 mm bore length; one receives a 33.87 mm-radius pin 175 mm long (79b03e80), and the other receives a 33.87 mm-radius pin 100 mm long (79af53d4), both with zero perpendicular misalignment and axial overlaps of about 10.6 mm, indicating tight coaxial fits. The smaller gear (79afa20a, 1 instance) hosts a 28.8 mm-diameter through-hole of 30 mm length that accepts a 14.4 mm-radius pin 120 mm long (79b1021e), again with perfect coaxial alignment (perp distance 0.0, axial overlap 30.6 mm). In total, face-to-face contacts break down as 4 coaxial cylindrical fits and 1 mating planar interface, plus 3 hole-inferred fastener-through-hole links. There is 1 ambiguous contact edge, likely at the tooth-mesh zone where conical gear surfaces interact. The surface-type histogram of 312 planar faces and 146 cylindrical faces reflects the many flat tooth flanks and the cylindrical bores and shafts.

Viewed from a slight isometric angle in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented grapple with a tapered central hub and four evenly spaced curved jaw arms fanning outward and downward; short cylindrical barrels and slender rods bridge between the hub and the mid-sections of each jaw. The single grounded central body (ae62e424, 1 instance) is a bell-shaped end fitting with a lower cylinder of radius 13.5 mm, a conical transition, an upper cylinder of radius 9.39 mm, and an eye lug bearing a 5 mm through-bore at the crown. Wrapping around the lower cylindrical section are eight thin mounting lugs (ae615d7e, 8 instances, aspect ratio 5.77) each carrying a 2.8 mm through-hole for pin retention; four D-shaped lug tabs (ae59bc4c, 4 instances) and four additional mounting lugs (ae6184a4, 4 instances) similarly interface with the upper cylinder, carrying 1.28 mm and 2.48 mm through-holes respectively. The jaw arms come in two mirrored variants — gripper tines (ae4cc3e8, 2 instances) and gripper jaws (ae7c8674, 2 instances) — each roughly 73.6 mm long with B-spline-curved profiles, longitudinal stiffening ribs, and two orthogonally oriented 3.6 mm pin holes at the pivot and mid-body positions. Four hollow cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a, 4 instances, ~28 mm long, 0.7 mm wall, domed end) span between the hub and jaw pivot zones; four follower rods (ae61f99c, 4 instances) with mushroom-head caps and transverse 1.2 mm bored bosses provide the linkage connection. Fasteners comprise four stepped pins (ae594702, shank dia 2.7 mm, head dia 3.6 mm), four flanged pins (ae596e1c, shaft dia 1.2 mm), four spool pins (ae610f34, shaft dia 1.2 mm), and four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a, dia ~1.08 mm). All 26 hole-link inferences show near-zero perpendicular distance (≤ 0.016 mm), confirming tight coaxial alignment. Layout analysis identifies 4-fold rotational symmetry about the Y-axis for eight different part classes and 4 mirror pairs corresponding to the two jaw variants and their associated curved support arms (ae44d4ac, ae6666ac, 2 instances each).

Viewed from a slight isometric angle in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented grapple with a tapered central hub and four evenly spaced curved jaw arms fanning outward and downward; short cylindrical barrels and slender rods bridge between the hub and the mid-sections of each jaw. The single grounded central body (ae62e424, 1 instance) is a bell-shaped end fitting with a lower cylinder of radius 13.5 mm, a conical transition, an upper cylinder of radius 9.39 mm, and an eye lug bearing a 5 mm through-bore at the crown. Wrapping around the lower cylindrical section are eight thin mounting lugs (ae615d7e, 8 instances, aspect ratio 5.77) each carrying a 2.8 mm through-hole for pin retention; four D-shaped lug tabs (ae59bc4c, 4 instances) and four additional mounting lugs (ae6184a4, 4 instances) similarly interface with the upper cylinder, carrying 1.28 mm and 2.48 mm through-holes respectively. The jaw arms come in two mirrored variants — gripper tines (ae4cc3e8, 2 instances) and gripper jaws (ae7c8674, 2 instances) — each roughly 73.6 mm long with B-spline-curved profiles, longitudinal stiffening ribs, and two orthogonally oriented 3.6 mm pin holes at the pivot and mid-body positions. Four hollow cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a, 4 instances, ~28 mm long, 0.7 mm wall, domed end) span between the hub and jaw pivot zones; four follower rods (ae61f99c, 4 instances) with mushroom-head caps and transverse 1.2 mm bored bosses provide the linkage connection. Fasteners comprise four stepped pins (ae594702, shank dia 2.7 mm, head dia 3.6 mm), four flanged pins (ae596e1c, shaft dia 1.2 mm), four spool pins (ae610f34, shaft dia 1.2 mm), and four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a, dia ~1.08 mm). All 26 hole-link inferences show near-zero perpendicular distance (≤ 0.016 mm), confirming tight coaxial alignment. Layout analysis identifies 4-fold rotational symmetry about the Y-axis for eight different part classes and 4 mirror pairs corresponding to the two jaw variants and their associated curved support arms (ae44d4ac, ae6666ac, 2 instances each).

The contact_edge_counts dictionary is empty, so all inter-part connectivity is inferred solely from the 26 hole-link edges; no conventional planar or cylindrical mating contacts were recorded. The 5 mm bore in the eye lug of the end fitting (ae62e424) and the ~0.86 mm radial holes on the cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a) have no matching fastener in the hole_edges_summary, meaning either no fastener body is modeled or the match fell outside detection thresholds. The four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a) likewise appear in no hole-link cluster. All 49 instances are marked grounded, preventing any kinematic inference from the assembly tree; the flat tree depth of 1 indicates no sub-assembly grouping.

Viewed from a slight isometric angle in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented grapple with a tapered central hub and four evenly spaced curved jaw arms fanning outward and downward; short cylindrical barrels and slender rods bridge between the hub and the mid-sections of each jaw. The single grounded central body (ae62e424, 1 instance) is a bell-shaped end fitting with a lower cylinder of radius 13.5 mm, a conical transition, an upper cylinder of radius 9.39 mm, and an eye lug bearing a 5 mm through-bore at the crown. Wrapping around the lower cylindrical section are eight thin mounting lugs (ae615d7e, 8 instances, aspect ratio 5.77) each carrying a 2.8 mm through-hole for pin retention; four D-shaped lug tabs (ae59bc4c, 4 instances) and four additional mounting lugs (ae6184a4, 4 instances) similarly interface with the upper cylinder, carrying 1.28 mm and 2.48 mm through-holes respectively. The jaw arms come in two mirrored variants — gripper tines (ae4cc3e8, 2 instances) and gripper jaws (ae7c8674, 2 instances) — each roughly 73.6 mm long with B-spline-curved profiles, longitudinal stiffening ribs, and two orthogonally oriented 3.6 mm pin holes at the pivot and mid-body positions. Four hollow cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a, 4 instances, ~28 mm long, 0.7 mm wall, domed end) span between the hub and jaw pivot zones; four follower rods (ae61f99c, 4 instances) with mushroom-head caps and transverse 1.2 mm bored bosses provide the linkage connection. Fasteners comprise four stepped pins (ae594702, shank dia 2.7 mm, head dia 3.6 mm), four flanged pins (ae596e1c, shaft dia 1.2 mm), four spool pins (ae610f34, shaft dia 1.2 mm), and four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a, dia ~1.08 mm). All 26 hole-link inferences show near-zero perpendicular distance (≤ 0.016 mm), confirming tight coaxial alignment. Layout analysis identifies 4-fold rotational symmetry about the Y-axis for eight different part classes and 4 mirror pairs corresponding to the two jaw variants and their associated curved support arms (ae44d4ac, ae6666ac, 2 instances each).

All 49 instances are marked grounded, preventing any kinematic inference from the assembly tree; the flat tree depth of 1 indicates no sub-assembly grouping.

Viewed from an isometric angle, the assembly presents as a shallow rectangular console (bbox 309 × 65 × 247 mm, aspect ratio ~4.75) with its major extent along the X-axis. The single grounded housing cover (300 × 230 × 45 mm, 157 planar + 126 cylindrical faces) forms the visible top deck containing arrays of rectangular cutouts with 1.0 mm corner fillets, into which 26 spacer-block instances (18.87 × 12.09 × 5.0 mm each, filleted-top rectangular prisms) are seated via plane-on-plane and cylinder-on-plane contacts, forming the raised tactile pads visible in groups across the surface. Beneath sits the base plate (309 × 245 × 28 mm, aspect ratio 11.1), a thin slab with elongated surface grooves and stepped edges that mates to the underside of the cover through 17 planar contacts. Two large disc-shaped caps (86 mm diameter, 20 mm tall), each bearing 14 circumferential slots and a small hemispherical indicator boss, sit in circular recesses and represent the jog-wheel elements—they connect via 2 plane-on-plane contacts each. Eleven solid cylindrical pins (diameter 1.5 mm, length 11 mm) protrude vertically through the top surface as control stems; no contact edges are recorded for these, suggesting press-fit or loose insertion into the socket posts. Eight bell-shaped socket posts (14.89 mm base diameter, 17 mm tall, with 1.178 mm through-holes) are distributed across the panel as receptacles. Six flanged bushings (flange diameter ~18.2 mm, central bore ~4.92 mm) likely serve as the front-panel jack connectors visible in the render. Three wedge guide blocks and three structural bars are arranged in 3-fold rotational patterns about axes perpendicular to the panel plane, likely forming a central mixer-crossfader region. Two ball studs (spherical head diameter ~18.2 mm) and three locating pins (mushroom-headed, with perpendicular cross-holes) complete the assembly. Layout tags confirm: overall planar arrangement (normal along Y), two 3-fold rotational clusters, and 2 mirror pairs reflecting the left-right symmetry of the dual-deck layout.

Parts:
- housing_cover: A flat rectangular plate approximately 300 × 230 × 45 mm (aspect ratio ~6.67) with numerous rectangular cutouts across its top face, each filleted at R = 1.0 mm, and 45° chamfers along the upper perimeter edges. - base_plate: A thin rectangular plate with bounding-box extents of approximately 27.8 × 244.9 × 309.0 mm (aspect ratio ~11.1), featuring narrow elongated grooves on the top surface, stepped protruding tabs along two lower edges, and a corner notch with 45° chamfers. It contains two blind holes (diameters ~6.9 mm and ~8.9 mm) that could receive locating pins or fasteners. - cap (×2): A squat circular disc approximately 86 mm in diameter and 20 mm tall (aspect ratio ~4.3), featuring 14 evenly spaced elongated slots around its cylindrical periphery, a shallow circular step (Ø 60.5 mm, 1 mm deep) that creates a raised central plateau, and a small hemispherical boss on the top surface. A tapered conical skirt widens the base, and three planar faces provide flat top and bottom mating surfaces. The part participates in 2 Plane-on-Plane contacts and appears in two instances within the assembly. - socket_post (×8): A bell-shaped conical post approximately 14.9 × 14.9 × 17.0 mm (aspect ratio 1.14) with a broad circular base flange at radius 7.45 mm and a tapering body formed by four conical surfaces. At the narrower top, a through-hole of diameter ~1.18 mm runs axially, bordered by a small freeform lug/tab feature that could provide retention for an inserted pin. Eight instances are present in the assembly, all grounded, suggesting these parts likely serve as fixed socket-type receptacle posts arranged around a structure.
- wedge_guide_block (×3): A small solid trapezoidal wedge (bbox ~8 × 14 × 18 mm, aspect ratio 2.25) composed entirely of 12 planar faces and 30 straight edges. The cross-section tapers from a flat base upward to a narrower top, where a longitudinal groove with a flat bottom and two symmetric 45° side walls runs the full length. No holes or cylindrical features are present. With three instances in the assembly and no recorded contact pairs, the part likely serves as a guide or support wedge whose top groove could receive a thin mating element such as a blade or rail.
- structural_bar (×3): A plain rectangular bar with extents of approximately 1.10 × 2.00 × 20.00 and an aspect ratio of about 18.2, comprising 6 flat planar faces, 12 straight edges, and no holes or curved features. It participates in 18 plane-on-plane contacts across 3 instances in the assembly, indicating extensive face-to-face mating with neighboring parts. The part appears to serve as a structural bar or post, providing rigidity through direct planar abutment rather than fastened joints.
- pin (×11): A plain solid cylinder of radius 0.75 and length 11.0, giving an aspect ratio of about 7.3. It has no holes, grooves, or other features—just one cylindrical lateral face and two flat circular end caps. The part is instantiated 11 times in the assembly, and its simple slender geometry with no threads or keyways suggests it likely serves as a locating pin or dowel that fits into matching bores in adjacent components.
- locating_pin (×3): A compact axisymmetric body (~14.9 × 14.9 × 17.0 mm, aspect ratio 1.14) featuring a domed head formed by a B-spline surface, a conical taper transitioning to a narrower shank, and an axial slot near the shank tip. A perpendicular through-hole of 1.178 mm diameter passes through the shank, likely permitting a retaining wire or clip. The part appears in three grounded instances and, given the domed head, tapered centering shank, and retention hole, could serve as a locating or retaining pin.
- spacer_block (×26): A small solid rectangular block approximately 18.87 × 12.09 × 5.0 mm (aspect ratio ~3.78) with 2.0 mm-radius rounded edges blending the flat top into the four vertical sides, producing a smooth pillow-top profile. It has 6 planar faces and 4 cylindrical fillet faces, no holes or internal features. Used 26 times in the assembly with 130 plane-on-plane and 52 cylinder-on-plane contacts, it likely serves as a repeated spacer or filler block that mates via its flat bottom and side faces against neighbouring planar surfaces.
- ball_stud (×2): A compact axisymmetric part (bbox ~18.2 × 18.2 × 10.0 mm, aspect ratio 1.82) featuring a large spherical dome head formed by B-spline surfaces and a cylindrical collar with a central through-bore of approximately 4.92 mm diameter extending 8 mm axially. Flat annular faces perpendicular to the axis participate in 2 plane-on-plane contacts in the assembly. Two instances exist, at least one grounded. The part likely serves as a ball stud or spherical pivot, where the dome could articulate against a mating socket and the bore permits attachment via a pin or fastener.
- flanged_bushing (×6): A small flanged body approximately 10 mm deep with a ~18.2 mm diameter flange and a cylindrical boss (radius ~5.03 mm, 5 mm long) on one side, plus a central bore of radius ~2.46 mm extending 8 mm axially. It features a conical countersink at the bore entry, fillet blends, and freeform flange surfaces. Six plane-on-plane contacts and six instances in the assembly indicate its flange seats against flat mating surfaces. This part likely serves as a flanged bushing or locating sleeve that positions relative to a mating bore while permitting a shaft to pass through its central bore.

Annotation Caveats:
No fastener-through-hole stacks were detected (hole_edge_count = 0), so all retention between parts is inferred as press-fit, snap, or adhesive. The 11 pin instances record zero contact edges despite clearly protruding through the panel, suggesting their cylindrical surfaces may not meet the contact-detection threshold or they are loosely inserted. The blind holes on the housing cover and base plate share matching diameters (6.924 mm and 8.91 mm) but are not linked by any contact edge, leaving their mutual alignment as inferred rather than confirmed. Several parts (socket_posts, locating_pins, wedge_guide_blocks) show zero contact edges in their dossiers, which may reflect modeling gaps or clearance fits below the contact tolerance.

The cross-section tapers from a flat base upward to a narrower top, where a longitudinal groove with a flat bottom and two symmetric 45° side walls runs the full length. No holes or cylindrical features are present. With three instances in the assembly and no recorded contact pairs, the part likely serves as a guide or support wedge whose top groove could receive a thin mating element such as a blade or rail.
- structural_bar (×3): A plain rectangular bar with extents of approximately 1.10 × 2.00 × 20.00 and an aspect ratio of about 18.2, comprising 6 flat planar faces, 12 straight edges, and no holes or curved features. It participates in 18 plane-on-plane contacts across 3 instances in the assembly, indicating extensive face-to-face mating with neighboring parts. The part appears to serve as a structural bar or post, providing rigidity through direct planar abutment rather than fastened joints.
- pin (×11): A plain solid cylinder of radius 0.75 and length 11.0, giving an aspect ratio of about 7.3. It has no holes, grooves, or other features—just one cylindrical lateral face and two flat circular end caps. The part is instantiated 11 times in the assembly, and its simple slender geometry with no threads or keyways suggests it likely serves as a locating pin or dowel that fits into matching bores in adjacent components.
- locating_pin (×3): A compact axisymmetric body (~14.9 × 14.9 × 17.0 mm, aspect ratio 1.14) featuring a domed head formed by a B-spline surface, a conical taper transitioning to a narrower shank, and an axial slot near the shank tip. A perpendicular through-hole of 1.178 mm diameter passes through the shank, likely permitting a retaining wire or clip. The part appears in three grounded instances and, given the domed head, tapered centering shank, and retention hole, could serve as a locating or retaining pin.
- spacer_block (×26): A small solid rectangular block approximately 18.87 × 12.09 × 5.0 mm (aspect ratio ~3.78) with 2.0 mm-radius rounded edges blending the flat top into the four vertical sides, producing a smooth pillow-top profile. It has 6 planar faces and 4 cylindrical fillet faces, no holes or internal features. Used 26 times in the assembly with 130 plane-on-plane and 52 cylinder-on-plane contacts, it likely serves as a repeated spacer or filler block that mates via its flat bottom and side faces against neighbouring planar surfaces.
- ball_stud (×2): A compact axisymmetric part (bbox ~18.2 × 18.2 × 10.0 mm, aspect ratio 1.82) featuring a large spherical dome head formed by B-spline surfaces and a cylindrical collar with a central through-bore of approximately 4.92 mm diameter extending 8 mm axially. Flat annular faces perpendicular to the axis participate in 2 plane-on-plane contacts in the assembly. Two instances exist, at least one grounded. The part likely serves as a ball stud or spherical pivot, where the dome could articulate against a mating socket and the bore permits attachment via a pin or fastener.
- flanged_bushing (×6): A small flanged body approximately 10 mm deep with a ~18.2 mm diameter flange and a cylindrical boss (radius ~5.03 mm, 5 mm long) on one side, plus a central bore of radius ~2.46 mm extending 8 mm axially. It features a conical countersink at the bore entry, fillet blends, and freeform flange surfaces. Six plane-on-plane contacts and six instances in the assembly indicate its flange seats against flat mating surfaces. This part likely serves as a flanged bushing or locating sleeve that positions relative to a mating bore while permitting a shaft to pass through its central bore.

Annotation Caveats:
No fastener-through-hole stacks were detected (hole_edge_count = 0), so all retention between parts is inferred as press-fit, snap, or adhesive. The 11 pin instances record zero contact edges despite clearly protruding through the panel, suggesting their cylindrical surfaces may not meet the contact-detection threshold or they are loosely inserted. The blind holes on the housing cover and base plate share matching diameters (6.924 mm and 8.91 mm) but are not linked by any contact edge, leaving their mutual alignment as inferred rather than confirmed. Several parts (socket_posts, locating_pins, wedge_guide_blocks) show zero contact edges in their dossiers, which may reflect modeling gaps or clearance fits below the contact tolerance.
